text.NRSV: The word of the Lord that came to Hosea son of Beeri, in the days of Kings Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah of Judah, and in the days of King Jeroboam son of Joash of Israel.
text.Rotherham: The word of Yahweh which came unto Hosea son of Beeri, in the days of Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, Hezekiah, kings of Judah,—and in the days of Jeroboam son of Joash, king of Israel.
text.KJV: The word of the Lord that came unto Hosea, the son of Beeri, in the days of Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, and Hezekiah, kings of Judah, and in the days of Jeroboam the son of Joash, king of Israel.
text.NABRE: The word of the Lord that came to Hosea son of Beeri, in the days of Uzziah, Jotham, Ahaz, Hezekiah, kings of Judah, and in the days of Jeroboam, son of Joash, king of Israel.
text.DRB: The word of our Lord, that was made to Osee the son of Beeri, in the days of Ozias, Joathan, Achaz, Ezechias, kings of Juda, and in the days of Jeroboam the son of Joas king of Israel.

text.NRSV: When the Lord first spoke through Hosea, the Lord said to Hosea, “Go, take for yourself a wife of whoredom and have children of whoredom, for the land commits great whoredom by forsaking the Lord.”
text.Rotherham: The beginning of the word of Yahweh with Hosea, was,—that Yahweh said unto Hosea, Go take thee a woman of unchastity, and the children of unchastity, for, unchastely indeed, hath the land been going away from following Yahweh.
text.KJV: The beginning of the word of the Lord by Hosea. And the Lord said to Hosea, Go, take unto thee a wife of whoredoms and children of whoredoms: for the land hath committed great whoredom, departing from the Lord.
text.NABRE: When the Lord began to speak with Hosea, the Lord said to Hosea: Go, get for yourself a woman of prostitution and children of prostitution, for the land prostitutes itself, turning away from the Lord.
text.DRB: The beginning to our Lord of speaking in Osee: and our Lord said to Osee: Go, take thee a wife of fornications, and make children of fornications: because the land fornicating shall fornicate from the Lord.

text.NRSV: So he went and took Gomer daughter of Diblaim, and she conceived and bore him a son.
text.Rotherham: So he went and took Gomer, daughter of Diblaim,—and she conceived and bare him a son.
text.KJV: So he went and took Gomer the daughter of Diblaim; which conceived, and bare him a son.
text.NABRE: So he went and took Gomer, daughter of Diblaim; and she conceived and bore him a son.
text.DRB: And he went, and took Gomer the daughter of Debelaim: and she conceived, and bare him a son.

text.NRSV: And the Lord said to him, “Name him Jezreel; for in a little while I will punish the house of Jehu for the blood of Jezreel, and I will put an end to the kingdom of the house of Israel.
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto him, Call his name Jezreel: for, yet a little, and I will visit the bloodshed of Jezreel upon the house of Jehu, and will cause to cease the kingdom of the house of Israel.
text.KJV: And the Lord said unto him, Call his name Jezreel; for yet a little while, and I will avenge the blood of Jezreel upon the house of Jehu, and will cause to cease the kingdom of the house of Israel.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord said to him: Give him the name “Jezreel,” for in a little while I will punish the house of Jehu for the bloodshed at Jezreel and bring to an end the kingdom of the house of Israel;
text.DRB: And our Lord said to him: Call his name Jezrahel: because yet a little while, and I will visit the blood of Jezrahel upon the house of Jehu, and I will make the kingdom of the house of Israel to cease.

text.NRSV: On that day I will break the bow of Israel in the valley of Jezreel.”
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass, in that day, that I will break the bow of Israel, in the vale of Jezreel.
text.KJV: And it shall come to pass at that day, that I will break the bow of Israel in the valley of Jezreel.
text.NABRE: on that day I will break the bow of Israel in the valley of Jezreel.
text.DRB: And in that day I will break the bow of Israel in the valley of Jezrahel.

text.NRSV: She conceived again and bore a daughter. Then the Lord said to him, “Name her Lo-ruhamah, for I will no longer have pity on the house of Israel or forgive them.
text.Rotherham: Then conceived she again and bare a daughter, and he said to him, Call her name Lo-ruhamah ["Uncompassionated"],—for, not again any more, will I have compassion upon the house of Israel, that I should, forgive, them;
text.KJV: And she conceived again, and bare a daughter. And God said unto him, Call her name Lo–ruhamah: for I will no more have mercy upon the house of Israel; but I will utterly take them away.
text.NABRE: She conceived again and bore a daughter. The Lord said to him: Give her the name “Not-Pitied,” for I will no longer feel pity for the house of Israel: rather, I will utterly abhor them.
text.DRB: And she conceived yet again, and bare a daughter, and he said to him: Call her name, Without mercy: because I will add no more to have mercy on the house of Israel, but with oblivion I will forget them.

text.NRSV: But I will have pity on the house of Judah, and I will save them by the Lord their God; I will not save them by bow, or by sword, or by war, or by horses, or by horsemen.”
text.Rotherham: but, on the house of Judah, will I have compassion, and I will save them, as Yahweh their God,—but will not save them by bow, or by sword, or by battle, by horses, or by horsemen.
text.KJV: But I will have mercy upon the house of Judah, and will save them by the Lord their God, and will not save them by bow, nor by sword, nor by battle, by horses, nor by horsemen.
text.NABRE: Yet for the house of Judah I will feel pity; I will save them by the Lord, their God; but I will not save them by bow or sword, by warfare, by horses or horsemen.
text.DRB: And I will have mercy on the house of Juda, and will save them in the Lord their God: and I will not save them in bow and sword, and in battle, & in horses, and in horsemen.

text.NRSV: When she had weaned Lo-ruhamah, she conceived and bore a son.
text.Rotherham: And, when she had weaned Lo-ruhamah, she conceived, and bare a son.
text.KJV: Now when she had weaned Lo–ruhamah, she conceived, and bare a son.
text.NABRE: After she weaned Not-Pitied, she conceived and bore a son.
text.DRB: And she weaned her that was, Without mercy. And she conceived, and bare a son.

text.NRSV: Then the Lord said, “Name him Lo-ammi, for you are not my people and I am not your God.”
text.Rotherham: Then said he, Call his name Lo-ammi [="No people of mine"],—for ye are Lo-ammi ["No people of mine"], and, I, will not be yours.
text.KJV: Then said God, Call his name Lo–ammi: for ye are not my people, and I will not be your God.
text.NABRE: Then the Lord said: Give him the name “Not-My-People,” for you are not my people, and I am not “I am” for you.
text.DRB: And he said: Call his name, Not my people: because you not my people, and I will not be yours.

text.NRSV: Yet the number of the people of Israel shall be like the sand of the sea, which can be neither measured nor numbered; and in the place where it was said to them, “You are not my people,” it shall be said to them, “Children of the living God.”
text.Rotherham: Yet shall the number of the sons of Israel become like the sand of the sea, which can neither he measured, nor numbered,—and it shall come to pass, in the place where it used to be said to them, No people of mine, are ye, it shall be said to them, Sons of a Living God!
text.KJV: Yet the number of the children of Israel shall be as the sand of the sea, which cannot be measured nor numbered; and it shall come to pass, that in the place where it was said unto them, Ye are not my people, there it shall be said unto them, Ye are the sons of the living God.
text.DRB: And the number of the children of Israel shall be as the sand of the sea, that is without measure, and shall not be numbered. And it shall be in place where it shall be said to them: Not my people you: it shall be said to them: Children of the living God.

text.NRSV: The people of Judah and the people of Israel shall be gathered together, and they shall appoint for themselves one head; and they shall take possession of the land, for great shall be the day of Jezreel.
text.Rotherham: Then shall the sons of Judah and the sons of Israel, gather themselves together, as one, and shall appoint them one head, and come up out of the earth,—for great shall be the day of Jezreel.
text.KJV: Then shall the children of Judah and the children of Israel be gathered together, and appoint themselves one head, and they shall come up out of the land: for great shall be the day of Jezreel.
text.DRB: And the children of Juda, and the children of Israel shall be gathered together: and they shall set to themselves, one head, and shall ascend out of the earth: because great is the day of Jezrahel.

text.NRSV: Say to your brother, Ammi, and to your sister, Ruhamah.
text.Rotherham: Say ye unto your brethren, O Ammi [="O my people"], and unto your sisters, O Ruhamah [="O compassionated one"]:
text.KJV: Say ye unto your brethren, Ammi; and to your sisters, Ru–hamah.
text.NABRE: Instead of being told, “You are Not-My-People,” They will be called, “Children of the living God.”
text.DRB: Say ye to your brethren: My people: and to your sister, She that hath obtained mercy.

text.NRSV: Plead with your mother, plead— for she is not my wife, and I am not her husband— that she put away her whoring from her face, and her adultery from between her breasts,
text.Rotherham: Contend ye with your mother, contend, for she is no wife of mine, and I am no husband of hers, Let her then put away her paramours from before her, and her partners in adultery, from her embraces:
text.KJV: Plead with your mother, plead: for she is not my wife, neither am I her husband: let her therefore put away her whoredoms out of her sight, and her adulteries from between her breasts;
text.NABRE: Then the people of Judah and of Israel will gather together; They will appoint for themselves one head and rise up from the land; great indeed shall be the day of Jezreel!
text.DRB: Judge your mother, judge ye: because she not my wife, and I not her husband. Let her take away fornications from her face, and her adulteries from the midst of her breasts.

text.NRSV: or I will strip her naked and expose her as on the day she was born, and make her like a wilderness, and turn her into a parched land, and kill her with thirst.
text.Rotherham: Lest I strip off her under-clothing, and set her forth to view, as in the day she was born,—and make her like a wilderness, and render her like a land that is parched, and suffer her to die of thirst;
text.KJV: Lest I strip her naked, and set her as in the day that she was born, and make her as a wilderness, and set her like a dry land, and slay her with thirst.
text.NABRE: Say to your brothers, “My People,” and to your sisters, “Pitied.”
text.DRB: Lest perhaps I strip her naked, and set her according to the day of her nativity: and I will lay her as a wilderness, and will set her as a land unpassable, and will kill her with drought.

text.NRSV: Upon her children also I will have no pity, because they are children of whoredom.
text.Rotherham: And, on her children, not have compassion,—because, the children of paramours, they are.
text.KJV: And I will not have mercy upon her children; for they be the children of whoredoms.
text.NABRE: The Lord and Israel His Spouse Accuse your mother, accuse! for she is not my wife, and I am not her husband. Let her remove her prostitution from her face. her adultery from between her breasts,
text.DRB: And I will not have mercy on her children: because they are the children of fornications:

text.NRSV: For their mother has played the whore; she who conceived them has acted shamefully. For she said, “I will go after my lovers; they give me my bread and my water, my wool and my flax, my oil and my drink.”
text.Rotherham: For their mother, hath been unchaste, and she that conceived them, hath caused shame,—for she said, Let me go after my lovers! who used to give my bread, and my water, my wool, and my flax, mine oil, and my drink.
text.KJV: For their mother hath played the harlot: she that conceived them hath done shamefully: for she said, I will go after my lovers, that give me my bread and my water, my wool and my flax, mine oil and my drink.
text.NABRE: Or I will strip her naked, leaving her as on the day of her birth; I will make her like the wilderness, make her like an arid land, and let her die of thirst.
text.DRB: because their mother hath fornicated, she is confounded that conceived them: because she said: I will go after my lovers, that give me my breads, & my waters, my wool, and my flax, mine oil, & my drink.

text.NRSV: Therefore I will hedge up her way with thorns; and I will build a wall against her, so that she cannot find her paths.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, behold me! hedging up her way, with thorns,—and I will wall her in, and, her footpaths, shall she not find.
text.KJV: Therefore, behold, I will hedge up thy way with thorns, and make a wall, that she shall not find her paths.
text.NABRE: I will have no pity on her children, for they are children of prostitution.
text.DRB: For this behold I will hedge thy way with thorns, and I will hedge it with a wall, & she shall not find her paths.

text.NRSV: She shall pursue her lovers, but not overtake them; and she shall seek them, but shall not find them. Then she shall say, “I will go and return to my first husband, for it was better with me then than now.”
text.Rotherham: And, when she shall pursue her lovers, and not overtake them, and shall seek them and not find, then will she say, Let me go my way now! and return unto my first husband, for it was better with me, then, than, now!
text.KJV: And she shall follow after her lovers, but she shall not overtake them; and she shall seek them, but shall not find them: then shall she say, I will go and return to my first husband; for then was it better with me than now.
text.NABRE: Yes, their mother has prostituted herself; she who conceived them has acted shamefully. For she said, “I will go after my lovers, who give me my bread and my water, my wool and my flax, my oil and my drink.”
text.DRB: And she shall follow her lovers, and shall not overtake them: and she shall seek them, and shall not find, and she shall say: I will go, and will return to my former husband: because it was well with me then, more than now.

text.NRSV: She did not know that it was I who gave her the grain, the wine, and the oil, and who lavished upon her silver and gold that they used for Baal.
text.Rotherham: But, she, owneth not, that, I, gave her—the corn, and the new wine and the oil,—silver, also increased I unto her, and gold—[which] they offered to Baal!
text.KJV: For she did not know that I gave her corn, and wine, and oil, and multiplied her silver and gold, which they prepared for Baal.
text.NABRE: Therefore, I will hedge in her way with thorns and erect a wall against her, so that she cannot find her paths.
text.DRB: And she did not know that I gave her corn, and wine, and oil, and multiplied silver unto her, & gold, which they have made to Baal.

text.NRSV: Therefore I will take back my grain in its time, and my wine in its season; and I will take away my wool and my flax, which were to cover her nakedness.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, will I again take away my corn, in the time thereof, and my new wine, in the season thereof,—and will recover my wool and my flax [given] to hide her shame.
text.KJV: Therefore will I return, and take away my corn in the time thereof, and my wine in the season thereof, and will recover my wool and my flax given to cover her nakedness.
text.NABRE: If she runs after her lovers, she will not overtake them; if she seeks them she will not find them. Then she will say, “I will go back to my first husband, for I was better off then than now.”
text.DRB: Therefore will I return, and will take my corn in his time, and my wine in his time, and I will deliver my wool, and my flax, which covered her ignominy.

text.NRSV: Now I will uncover her shame in the sight of her lovers, and no one shall rescue her out of my hand.
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore, will I expose her unseemliness, before the eyes of her lovers,—and no, man, shall deliver her out of my hand!
text.KJV: And now will I discover her lewdness in the sight of her lovers, and none shall deliver her out of mine hand.
text.NABRE: She did not know that it was I who gave her the grain, the wine, and the oil, I who lavished upon her silver, and gold, which they used for Baal,
text.DRB: And now I will reveal her folly in the eyes of her lovers: and there shall not a man deliver her out of my hand:

text.NRSV: I will put an end to all her mirth, her festivals, her new moons, her sabbaths, and all her appointed feasts.
text.Rotherham: And I will cause to cease all her mirth, her pilgrim-festival, her new moon and her sabbath,—and her every appointed meeting;
text.KJV: I will also cause all her mirth to cease, her feast days, her new moons, and her sabbaths, and all her solemn feasts.
text.NABRE: Therefore I will take back my grain in its time, and my wine in its season; I will snatch away my wool and my flax, which were to cover her nakedness.
text.DRB: and I will make all her joy to cease, her solemnity, her new-moon, her sabbath, & all her festival times.

text.NRSV: I will lay waste her vines and her fig trees, of which she said, “These are my pay, which my lovers have given me.” I will make them a forest, and the wild animals shall devour them.
text.Rotherham: And will lay waste her vine and her fig- tree, as to which she hath said, A present, are they for myself, which my lovers, have given me,—and I will make of them a thicket, and the wild beasts of the field shall devour them.
text.KJV: And I will destroy her vines and her fig trees, whereof she hath said, These are my rewards that my lovers have given me: and I will make them a forest, and the beasts of the field shall eat them.
text.NABRE: Now I will lay bare her shame in full view of her lovers, and no one can deliver her out of my hand.
text.DRB: And I will destroy her vine, and her figtree, of which she said: These are my rewards, which my lovers have given me: and I will lay her as a forest, & the beast of the field shall eat her.

text.NRSV: I will punish her for the festival days of the Baals, when she offered incense to them and decked herself with her rings and jewelry, and went after her lovers, and forgot me, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: So will I visit upon her the days of the Baals, unto whom she used to burn incense, and decked herself with her nose-ring and her jewelry, and went her way after her lovers,—whereas, me, she forgat, Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: And I will visit upon her the days of Baalim, wherein she burned incense to them, and she decked herself with her earrings and her jewels, and she went after her lovers, and forgat me, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: I will put an end to all her joy, her festivals, her new moons, her sabbaths— all her seasonal feasts.
text.DRB: And I will visit upon her the days of Baalim, to whom she burnt incense, and was adorned with her earlet, and with her tablet, & went after her lovers, & forgot me, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: Therefore, I will now allure her, and bring her into the wilderness, and speak tenderly to her.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, lo! I, am going to persuade her, and, though I conduct her forth into wilderness, yet will I speak unto her heart.
text.KJV: Therefore, behold, I will allure her, and bring her into the wilderness, and speak comfortably unto her.
text.NABRE: I will lay waste her vines and fig trees, of which she said, “These are the fees my lovers have given me”; I will turn them into rank growth and wild animals shall devour them.
text.DRB: For this, lo I will allure her, and will lead her into the wilderness: and I will speak to her heart.

text.NRSV: From there I will give her her vineyards, and make the Valley of Achor a door of hope. There she shall respond as in the days of her youth, as at the time when she came out of the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: Then will I give to her her vineyards from thence, and the vale of Achor [="trouble"] for a door of hope,—and she will respond there, as in the days of her youth, and as in the day when she came up out of the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: And I will give her her vineyards from thence, and the valley of Achor for a door of hope: and she shall sing there, as in the days of her youth, and as in the day when she came up out of the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: I will punish her for the days of the Baals, for whom she burnt incense, When she decked herself out with her rings and her jewelry, and went after her lovers— but me she forgot—oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: And I will give her dressers of vines out of the same place, and the vale of Achor to open hope: and she shall sing there according to the days of her youth, and according to the days of her ascending out of the Land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: On that day, says the Lord, you will call me, “My husband,” and no longer will you call me, “My Baal.”
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass, in that day, Declareth Yahweh, that she will call me Ishi [="My husband"], and will not call me any more, Baali [="Mine owner"].
text.KJV: And it shall be at that day, saith the Lord, that thou shalt call me Ishi; and shalt call me no more Baali.
text.NABRE: Therefore, I will allure her now; I will lead her into the wilderness and speak persuasively to her.
text.DRB: And it shall be in that day, saith our Lord: She shall call me: My husband, and she shall call me no more, Baalim.

text.NRSV: For I will remove the names of the Baals from her mouth, and they shall be mentioned by name no more.
text.Rotherham: So will I take away the names of the Baals, out of her mouth,—and they shall not be called to mind any more, by their name.
text.KJV: For I will take away the names of Baalim out of her mouth, and they shall no more be remembered by their name.
text.NABRE: Then I will give her the vineyards she had, and the valley of Achor as a door of hope. There she will respond as in the days of her youth, as on the day when she came up from the land of Egypt.
text.DRB: And I will take away the names of Baalim out of her mouth, and she shall no more remember their name.

text.NRSV: I will make for you a covenant on that day with the wild animals, the birds of the air, and the creeping things of the ground; and I will abolish the bow, the sword, and war from the land; and I will make you lie down in safety.
text.Rotherham: And I will solemnize to them a covenant, in that day, with the wild-beast of the field, and with the bird of the heavens, and the creeping thing of the ground,—and, bow and sword and battle, will I break in pieces out of the land, so will I cause them to lie down, in security.
text.KJV: And in that day will I make a covenant for them with the beasts of the field, and with the fowls of heaven, and with the creeping things of the ground: and I will break the bow and the sword and the battle out of the earth, and will make them to lie down safely.
text.NABRE: On that day—oracle of the Lord — You shall call me “My husband,” and you shall never again call me “My baal.”
text.DRB: And I will make with them a league in that day, with the beast of the field, and with the fowl of the heaven, and with that, which creepeth on the earth: and bow, and sword, and battle I will destroy out of the earth: and will make them sleep confidently.

text.NRSV: And I will take you for my wife forever; I will take you for my wife in righteousness and in justice, in steadfast love, and in mercy.
text.Rotherham: And I will take thee unto myself, unto times age-abiding,—yea I will take thee unto myself, in righteousness and in justice, and in lovingkindness, and in abounding compassion:
text.KJV: And I will betroth thee unto me for ever; yea, I will betroth thee unto me in righteousness, and in judgment, and in lovingkindness, and in mercies.
text.NABRE: I will remove from her mouth the names of the Baals; they shall no longer be mentioned by their name.
text.DRB: And I will despouse thee to me for ever: and I will despouse thee to me in justice, and judgement, and in mercy, and in commiserations.

text.NRSV: I will take you for my wife in faithfulness; and you shall know the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Yea I will take thee unto myself, in faithfulness,—So shalt thou know Yahweh.
text.KJV: I will even betroth thee unto me in faithfulness: and thou shalt know the Lord.
text.NABRE: I will make a covenant for them on that day, with the wild animals, With the birds of the air, and with the things that crawl on the ground. Bow and sword and warfare I will destroy from the land, and I will give them rest in safety.
text.DRB: And I will despouse thee to me in faith: and thou shalt know that I am the Lord.

text.NRSV: On that day I will answer, says the Lord, I will answer the heavens and they shall answer the earth;
text.Rotherham: And it shall come to pass in that day, that I will respond, Declareth Yahweh, I will respond to the heavens,—and, they, shall respond to the earth;
text.KJV: And it shall come to pass in that day, I will hear, saith the Lord, I will hear the heavens, and they shall hear the earth;
text.NABRE: I will betroth you to me forever: I will betroth you to me with justice and with judgment, with loyalty and with compassion;
text.DRB: And it shall be in that day: I will hear, saith our Lord, I will hear the heavens, and they shall hear the earth.

text.NRSV: and the earth shall answer the grain, the wine, and the oil, and they shall answer Jezreel;
text.Rotherham: And, the earth, shall respond to the corn, and to the new wine and to the oil,—and, they, shall respond to Jezreel [="Whom GOD scattereth," "Whom GOD soweth"].
text.KJV: And the earth shall hear the corn, and the wine, and the oil; and they shall hear Jezreel.
text.NABRE: I will betroth you to me with fidelity, and you shall know the Lord.
text.DRB: And the earth shall hear wheat, and wine, and oil: and these shall hear Jezrahel.

text.NRSV: and I will sow him for myself in the land. And I will have pity on Lo-ruhamah, and I will say to Lo-ammi, “You are my people”; and he shall say, “You are my God.”
text.Rotherham: So will I sow her unto me in the land, and will have compassion upon the uncompassionated one [="Lo-ruhamah"],—and will say to him who was No-people-of-mine [="Lo-ammi"], My people, thou art, and, he, shall say, My God!
text.KJV: And I will sow her unto me in the earth; and I will have mercy upon her that had not obtained mercy; and I will say to them which were not my people, Thou art my people; and they shall say, Thou art my God.
text.NABRE: On that day I will respond—oracle of the Lord — I will respond to the heavens, and they will respond to the earth;
text.DRB: And I will sow her unto me into the earth, and I will have mercy on her, that was without mercy.

text.NRSV: The Lord said to me again, “Go, love a woman who has a lover and is an adulteress, just as the Lord loves the people of Israel, though they turn to other gods and love raisin-cakes.”
text.Rotherham: Then said Yahweh unto me, Once more, go love a woman who loveth a friend, and is an adulteress,—according to the love of Yahweh unto the sons of Israel, though they keep turning away unto other gods, and love [idolatrous] raisin-cakes.
text.KJV: Then said the Lord unto me, Go yet, love a woman beloved of her friend, yet an adulteress, according to the love of the Lord toward the children of Israel, who look to other gods, and love flagons of wine.
text.NABRE: Hosea and His Wife Reunited Again the Lord said to me: Go, love a woman who is loved by her spouse but commits adultery; Just as the Lord loves the Israelites, though they turn to other gods and love raisin cakes.
text.DRB: And our Lord said to me: Yet again go, love a woman beloved of her friend, and an adulteress; as our Lord loveth the children of Israel, and they have respect to strange gods, and love the kernels of grapes.

text.NRSV: So I bought her for fifteen shekels of silver and a homer of barley and a measure of wine.
text.Rotherham: So I secured her to me, for fifteen pieces of silver,—and a homer of barley, and a half-homer of barley;
text.KJV: So I bought her to me for fifteen pieces of silver, and for an homer of barley, and an half homer of barley:
text.NABRE: So I acquired her for myself for fifteen pieces of silver and a homer and a lethech of barley.
text.DRB: And I digged her unto me for fifteen pieces of silver, and for a core of barley, and for half a core of barley.

text.NRSV: And I said to her, “You must remain as mine for many days; you shall not play the whore, you shall not have intercourse with a man, nor I with you.”
text.Rotherham: and I said unto her, Many days, shalt thou tarry for me, thou shalt not be unchaste, neither shalt thou become another man’s,—moreover also, I, [will tarry] for thee.
text.KJV: And I said unto her, Thou shalt abide for me many days; thou shalt not play the harlot, and thou shalt not be for another man: so will I also be for thee.
text.NABRE: Then I said to her: “You will wait for me for many days; you will not prostitute yourself Or belong to any man; I in turn will wait for you.”
text.DRB: And I said to her: Thou shalt expect me many days: thou shalt not fornicate, and thou shalt be no man's: but I also will expect thee.

text.NRSV: For the Israelites shall remain many days without king or prince, without sacrifice or pillar, without ephod or teraphim.
text.Rotherham: For, many days, shall the sons of Israel tarry, without king, and without ruler, and without sacrifice, and without pillar; and without ephod, or household gods.
text.KJV: For the children of Israel shall abide many days without a king, and without a prince, and without a sacrifice, and without an image, and without an ephod, and without teraphim:
text.NABRE: For the Israelites will remain many days without king or prince, Without sacrifice or sacred pillar, without ephod or household gods.
text.DRB: Because many days shall the children of Israel sit without king, and without prince, and without sacrifice, and without altar, and without ephod, and without theraphim.

text.NRSV: Afterward the Israelites shall return and seek the Lord their God, and David their king; they shall come in awe to the Lord and to his goodness in the latter days.
text.Rotherham: Afterwards, shall the sons of Israel return, and seek Yahweh their God, and David their king,—and shall turn with throbbing hearts unto Yahweh and unto his goodness, in the afterpart of the days.
text.KJV: Afterward shall the children of Israel return, and seek the Lord their God, and David their king; and shall fear the Lord and his goodness in the latter days.
text.NABRE: Afterward the Israelites will turn back and seek the Lord, their God, and David, their king; They will come trembling to the Lord and to his bounty, in the last days.
text.DRB: And after this the children of Israel shall return, and shall seek the Lord their God, and David their king: and they shall dread at the Lord, and at his goodness in the last days.

text.NRSV: Hear the word of the Lord, O people of Israel; for the Lord has an indictment against the inhabitants of the land. There is no faithfulness or loyalty, and no knowledge of God in the land.
text.Rotherham: Hear the word of Yahweh, ye sons of Israel,—that, a controversy, hath Yahweh with the inhabitants of the land, because there is no faithfulness, nor lovingkindness, nor knowledge of God, in the land:
text.KJV: Hear the word of the Lord, ye children of Israel: for the Lord hath a controversy with the inhabitants of the land, because there is no truth, nor mercy, nor knowledge of God in the land.
text.NABRE: Hear the word of the Lord, Israelites, for the Lord has a dispute with the inhabitants of the land: There is no fidelity, no loyalty, no knowledge of God in the land.
text.DRB: Hear the word of our Lord ye children of Israel, because there is judgement to our Lord with the inhabitants of the land: for there is no truth, and there is no mercy, and there is no knowledge of God in the land.

text.NRSV: Swearing, lying, and murder, and stealing and adultery break out; bloodshed follows bloodshed.
text.Rotherham: Cursing and lying, and killing and stealing, and committing adultery, have broken forth, and, blood-shedding, unto blood-shedding, doth extend.
text.KJV: By swearing, and lying, and killing, and stealing, and committing adultery, they break out, and blood toucheth blood.
text.NABRE: Swearing, lying, murder, stealing and adultery break out; bloodshed follows bloodshed.
text.DRB: Cursing, and lying, and manslaughter, & theft, and adultery have overflowed, & blood hath touched blood.

text.NRSV: Therefore the land mourns, and all who live in it languish; together with the wild animals and the birds of the air, even the fish of the sea are perishing.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, shall the land mourn, and everyone who dwelleth therein shall languish, with the wild beast of the field and with the bird of the heavens,—moreover also, the fishes of the sea, shall be withdrawn.
text.KJV: Therefore shall the land mourn, and every one that dwelleth therein shall languish, with the beasts of the field, and with the fowls of heaven; yea, the fishes of the sea also shall be taken away.
text.NABRE: Therefore the land dries up, and everything that dwells in it languishes: The beasts of the field, the birds of the air, and even the fish of the sea perish.
text.DRB: For this shall the land mourn, and every one shall be weakened that dwelleth in it, in the beast of the field, and in the fowl of the heaven: yea and the fishes of the sea shall be gathered together.

text.NRSV: Yet let no one complain, and let no one accuse, for with you is my contention, O priest.
text.Rotherham: Howbeit let, no man, contend, nor let him rebuke another,—since, thy people, are as they who contend against a priest:
text.KJV: Yet let no man strive, nor reprove another: for thy people are as they that strive with the priest.
text.NABRE: Guilt of Priest and of People But let no one accuse, let no one rebuke; with you is my dispute, priest!
text.DRB: But yet let not every man judge: and let not a man be rebuked: for the people are as those, that gainsay the Priest.

text.NRSV: You shall stumble by day; the prophet also shall stumble with you by night, and I will destroy your mother.
text.Rotherham: So shouldst thou stumble in the daytime, and even the prophet stumble with thee, in the night,—and I should destroy thine own mother.
text.KJV: Therefore shalt thou fall in the day, and the prophet also shall fall with thee in the night, and I will destroy thy mother.
text.NABRE: You will stumble in the day, and the prophet will stumble with you at night; I will make an end of your mother.
text.DRB: And thou shalt fall today, and the Prophet also shall fall with thee: in the night I made thy mother hold her peace.

text.NRSV: My people are destroyed for lack of knowledge; because you have rejected knowledge, I reject you from being a priest to me. And since you have forgotten the law of your God, I also will forget your children.
text.Rotherham: My people, are destroyed, for lack of knowledge,—Because, thou, hast rejected knowledge, therefore will I reject thee from ministering as priest unto me, and, because thou hast forgotten the law of thy God, I also, will forget thy children.
text.KJV: My people are destroyed for lack of knowledge: because thou hast rejected knowledge, I will also reject thee, that thou shalt be no priest to me: seeing thou hast forgotten the law of thy God, I will also forget thy children.
text.NABRE: My people are ruined for lack of knowledge! Since you have rejected knowledge, I will reject you from serving as my priest; Since you have forgotten the law of your God, I will also forget your children.
text.DRB: My people have held their peace, because they had not knowledge: because thou hast repelled knowledge, I will repel thee, that thou do not the function of priesthood unto me: and thou hast forgotten the law of thy God, I also will forget thy children.

text.NRSV: The more they increased, the more they sinned against me; they changed their glory into shame.
text.Rotherham: As they were magnified, so, they sinned against me,—My glory—for what was contemptible, did they exchange.
text.KJV: As they were increased, so they sinned against me: therefore will I change their glory into shame.
text.NABRE: The more they multiplied, the more they sinned against me, I will change their glory into shame.
text.DRB: According to the multitude of them so have they sinned to me: their glory I will change into ignominy.

text.NRSV: They feed on the sin of my people; they are greedy for their iniquity.
text.Rotherham: The sin of my people, they do eat,—and. unto their iniquity, lift they up every man his desire.
text.KJV: They eat up the sin of my people, and they set their heart on their iniquity.
text.NABRE: They feed on the sin of my people, and are greedy for their iniquity.
text.DRB: They shall eat the sins of my people, and at their iniquity shall lift up their souls.

text.NRSV: And it shall be like people, like priest; I will punish them for their ways, and repay them for their deeds.
text.Rotherham: So doth it come to be—like people, like priest,—therefore will I visit upon him his ways, and, his doings, will I bring back to him;
text.KJV: And there shall be, like people, like priest: and I will punish them for their ways, and reward them their doings.
text.NABRE: Like people, like priest: I will punish them for their ways, and repay them for their deeds.
text.DRB: And as the people, so shall the Priest be: and I will visit their ways upon them, and their cogitations I will render to them.

text.NRSV: They shall eat, but not be satisfied; they shall play the whore, but not multiply; because they have abandoned the Lord to devote themselves to
text.Rotherham: And they shall eat, and not be satisfied, they have encouraged unchastity, yet have not been making increase,—for unto, Yahweh, have they left off giving heed:—
text.KJV: For they shall eat, and not have enough: they shall commit whoredom, and shall not increase: because they have left off to take heed to the Lord.
text.NABRE: They will eat but not be satisfied, they will promote prostitution but not increase, Because they have abandoned the Lord, devoting themselves
text.DRB: And they shall eat and shall not be filled: they have fornicated, & have not ceased: because they have forsaken our Lord in not observing.

text.NRSV: whoredom. Wine and new wine take away the understanding.
text.Rotherham: Unchastity, and wine, and new wine, take away the heart.
text.KJV: Whoredom and wine and new wine take away the heart.
text.NABRE: to prostitution. Aged wine and new wine take away understanding.
text.DRB: Fornication, & wine, & drunkenness take away the heart.

text.NRSV: My people consult a piece of wood, and their divining rod gives them oracles. For a spirit of whoredom has led them astray, and they have played the whore, forsaking their God.
text.Rotherham: My people, of their Wood, do ask,—Let, their Staff, then tell them,—for, the spirit of unchastity, hath led them astray, and they have unchastely departed from under their God.
text.KJV: My people ask counsel at their stocks, and their staff declareth unto them: for the spirit of whoredoms hath caused them to err, and they have gone a whoring from under their God.
text.NABRE: My people consult their piece of wood, and their wand makes pronouncements for them, For the spirit of prostitution has led them astray; they prostitute themselves, forsaking their God.
text.DRB: My people hath asked in their wood, & their staff hath declared unto them: for the spirit of fornications hath deceived them, & they have fornicated from their God.

text.NRSV: They sacrifice on the tops of the mountains, and make offerings upon the hills, under oak, poplar, and terebinth, because their shade is good. Therefore your daughters play the whore, and your daughters-in-law commit adultery.
text.Rotherham: On the headlands of the mountains, they sacrifice, and, on the hills, burn they incense, under oak and poplar and terebinth, because, pleasant, is the shade thereof: For this cause, do your daughters, become unchaste, and, your brides, commit adultery.
text.KJV: They sacrifice upon the tops of the mountains, and burn incense upon the hills, under oaks and poplars and elms, because the shadow thereof is good: therefore your daughters shall commit whoredom, and your spouses shall commit adultery.
text.NABRE: On the mountaintops they offer sacrifice and on the hills they burn incense, Beneath oak and poplar and terebinth, because of their pleasant shade. Therefore your daughters prostitute themselves, and your daughters-in-law commit adultery.
text.DRB: Upon the heads of mountains they did sacrifice, and upon little hills they burnt incense: under the oak, and the poplar-tree, and the terebinth, because the shadow thereof was good: therefore shall your daughters fornicate, and your spouses shall be adulteresses.

text.NRSV: I will not punish your daughters when they play the whore, nor your daughters-in-law when they commit adultery; for the men themselves go aside with whores, and sacrifice with temple prostitutes; thus a people without understanding comes to ruin.
text.Rotherham: I do not bring punishment upon your daughters when they become unchaste, nor upon your brides, when they commit adultery, for, the men themselves, with unchaste women, do seclude themselves, and, with the common women of the shrine, do offer sacrifice,—and, a people who will not discern, must be ruined.
text.KJV: I will not punish your daughters when they commit whoredom, nor your spouses when they commit adultery: for themselves are separated with whores, and they sacrifice with harlots: therefore the people that doth not understand shall fall.
text.NABRE: I will not punish your daughters for their prostitution, nor your daughters-in-law for their adultery, Because the men themselves consort with prostitutes, and with temple women they offer sacrifice! Thus a people without understanding comes to ruin.
text.DRB: I will not visit upon your daughters when they shall fornicate, and upon your spouses when they shall commit adultery: because they themselves converst with harlots, and with the effeminate they did sacrifice, and the people not understanding shall be beaten.

text.NRSV: Though you play the whore, O Israel, do not let Judah become guilty. Do not enter Gilgal, or go up to Beth-aven, and do not swear, “As the Lord lives.”
text.Rotherham: Though unchaste art thou, O Israel, let not Judah, become guilty, neither let them enter Gilgal, nor go up to Beth-aven, and [then] swear, By the life of Yahweh!
text.KJV: Though thou, Israel, play the harlot, yet let not Judah offend; and come not ye unto Gilgal, neither go ye up to Beth–aven, nor swear, The Lord liveth.
text.NABRE: Though you prostitute yourself, Israel, do not let Judah become guilty! Do not come to Gilgal, do not go up to Beth-aven, do not swear, “As the Lord lives!”
text.DRB: If thou fornicate o Israel, at the least let not Juda offend: and enter ye not into Galgal, and go not up into Bethaven, neither swear ye: Our Lord liveth.

text.NRSV: Like a stubborn heifer, Israel is stubborn; can the Lord now feed them like a lamb in a broad pasture?
text.Rotherham: For, as a heifer that is stubborn, hath Israel, been stubborn,—Now, can Yahweh, turn them out to pasture, like a young ram in a wide place?
text.KJV: For Israel slideth back as a backsliding heifer: now the Lord will feed them as a lamb in a large place.
text.NABRE: For like a stubborn cow, Israel is stubborn; Will the Lord now pasture them, like lambs in a broad meadow?
text.DRB: Because Israel hath declined as a wanton cow: now will our Lord feed them, as a lamb in latitude.

text.NRSV: Ephraim is joined to idols—let him alone.
text.Rotherham: Mated with idols, is Ephraim, let him alone.
text.KJV: Ephraim is joined to idols: let him alone.
text.NABRE: Ephraim is bound to idols, let him alone!
text.DRB: Ephraim is partaker of idols, let him alone.

text.NRSV: When their drinking is over, they indulge in whoredom; they love lewdness more than their glory.
text.Rotherham: Their drinking-bout, having passed,—they became, unchaste, they loved wildly, a contempt, became her great men.
text.KJV: Their drink is sour: they have committed whoredom continually: her rulers with shame do love, Give ye.
text.NABRE: When their drinking is over, they give themselves to prostitution; they love shame more than their honor.
text.DRB: Their banquet is separated, with fornication they have fornicated: the protectors thereof loved to bring ignominy.

text.NRSV: A wind has wrapped them in its wings, and they shall be ashamed because of their sacrifices.
text.Rotherham: The wind hath bound her up in its wings,—that they may be ashamed, because of their sacrifices.
text.KJV: The wind hath bound her up in her wings, and they shall be ashamed because of their sacrifices.
text.NABRE: A wind has bound them up in its wings; they shall be ashamed because of their altars.
text.DRB: The spirit hath bound him in his wings, and they shall be confounded at their sacrifices.

text.NRSV: Hear this, O priests! Give heed, O house of Israel! Listen, O house of the king! For the judgment pertains to you; for you have been a snare at Mizpah, and a net spread upon Tabor,
text.Rotherham: Hear ye this—O priests, and attend, O house of Israel, and ye, House of the King, give ear, for, to you, pertaineth the sentence,—for, a snare, have ye been to Mizpah, and a net spread on Tabor.
text.KJV: Hear ye this, O priests; and hearken, ye house of Israel; and give ye ear, O house of the king; for judgment is toward you, because ye have been a snare on Mizpah, and a net spread upon Tabor.
text.NABRE: Hear this, priests, Pay attention, house of Israel, Household of the king, give ear! For you are responsible for judgment. But you have been a snare at Mizpah, a net spread upon Tabor,
text.DRB: Hear ye this o Priests, and attend ye house of Israel, and you the king's house harken: because there is judgement for you, because you are become a snare to speculation, & a net spread upon Thabor.

text.NRSV: and a pit dug deep in Shittim; but I will punish all of them.
text.Rotherham: And, a slaughter, have apostates deeply designed,—though, I, was a rebuker to them all.
text.KJV: And the revolters are profound to make slaughter, though I have been a rebuker of them all.
text.NABRE: a pit dug deep in Shittim. Now I will discipline them all.
text.DRB: And victims you have declined into the depth: & I the teacher of them all.

text.NRSV: I know Ephraim, and Israel is not hidden from me; for now, O Ephraim, you have played the whore; Israel is defiled.
text.Rotherham: I, have known Ephraim, and, Israel, hath not been hidden from me,—for, now, hast thou committed unchastity, O Ephraim, Israel, hath made himself impure.
text.KJV: I know Ephraim, and Israel is not hid from me: for now, O Ephraim, thou committest whoredom, and Israel is defiled.
text.NABRE: I know Ephraim, and Israel is not hidden from me: Now, Ephraim, you have practiced prostitution, Israel is defiled.
text.DRB: I know Ephraim, & Israel is not hid from me: because now hath Ephraim fornicated, Israel is contaminated.

text.NRSV: Their deeds do not permit them to return to their God. For the spirit of whoredom is within them, and they do not know the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Their doings, will not suffer, them to return unto their God,—for, the spirit of unchastity, is within them, and, Yahweh, have they not known.
text.KJV: They will not frame their doings to turn unto their God: for the spirit of whoredoms is in the midst of them, and they have not known the Lord.
text.NABRE: Their deeds do not allow them to return to their God; For the spirit of prostitution is in them, and they do not know the Lord.
text.DRB: They will not give their cogitations to return to their God, because the spirit of fornications is in the midst of them, & they have not known the Lord.

text.NRSV: Israel’s pride testifies against him; Ephraim stumbles in his guilt; Judah also stumbles with them.
text.Rotherham: Therefore will the Excellency of Israel, answer, to his face,—and, Israel and Ephraim, shall stumble in their iniquity, even Judah with them, hath stumbled.
text.KJV: And the pride of Israel doth testify to his face: therefore shall Israel and Ephraim fall in their iniquity; Judah also shall fall with them.
text.NABRE: The arrogance of Israel bears witness against him; Israel and Ephraim stumble because of their iniquity, and Judah stumbles with them.
text.DRB: And the arrogancy of Israel shall answer in his face: and Israel and Ephraim shall fall in their iniquity, Judas also shall fall with them.

text.NRSV: With their flocks and herds they shall go to seek the Lord, but they will not find him; he has withdrawn from them.
text.Rotherham: With their flocks and with their herds, will they go to seek Yahweh, but shall not find him; he hath withdrawn himself from them.
text.KJV: They shall go with their flocks and with their herds to seek the Lord; but they shall not find him; he hath withdrawn himself from them.
text.NABRE: With their flocks and herds they will go to seek the Lord, but will not find him; he has withdrawn from them.
text.DRB: In their flocks and in their herds they shall go to seek the Lord, and shall not find: he is taken away from them.

text.NRSV: They have dealt faithlessly with the Lord; for they have borne illegitimate children. Now the new moon shall devour them along with their parcels of land.
text.Rotherham: With Yahweh, have they dealt treacherously, for, to alien children, have they given birth,—now, a new moon, shall devour them, with their portions.
text.KJV: They have dealt treacherously against the Lord: for they have begotten strange children: now shall a month devour them with their portions.
text.NABRE: They have betrayed the Lord, for they have borne illegitimate children; Now the new moon will devour them together with their fields.
text.DRB: They have prevaricated against the Lord, because they have begotten strange children: now shall a month devour them with their parts.

text.NRSV: Blow the horn in Gibeah, the trumpet in Ramah. Sound the alarm at Beth-aven; look behind you, Benjamin!
text.Rotherham: Blow ye a horn in Gibeah, a trumpet in Ramah,—sound an alarm at Beth-aven, behind thee, O Benjamin!
text.KJV: Blow ye the cornet in Gibeah, and the trumpet in Ramah: cry aloud at Beth–aven, after thee, O Benjamin.
text.NABRE: Political Upheavals Blow the ram’s horn in Gibeah, the trumpet in Ramah! Sound the alarm in Beth-aven: “Look behind you, Benjamin!”
text.DRB: Sound with the trumpet in Gabaa, & with the shalm in Rama: howl ye in Bethaven, behind thy back o Benjamin.

text.NRSV: Ephraim shall become a desolation on the day of punishment; among the tribes of Israel I declare what is sure.
text.Rotherham: Ephraim, shall become, a desolation, in the day of rebuke: Throughout the tribes of Israel, have I hide known what is sure.
text.KJV: Ephraim shall be desolate in the day of rebuke: among the tribes of Israel have I made known that which shall surely be.
text.NABRE: Ephraim shall become a wasteland on the day of punishment: Among the tribes of Israel I announce what is sure to be.
text.DRB: Ephraim shall be in desolation in the day of correction: in the tribes of Israel I have shewed faith.

text.NRSV: The princes of Judah have become like those who remove the landmark; on them I will pour out my wrath like water.
text.Rotherham: The rulers of Judah have become as they who remove a land-mark. Upon them, will I pour out, like water, my wrath.
text.KJV: The princes of Judah were like them that remove the bound: therefore I will pour out my wrath upon them like water.
text.NABRE: The princes of Judah have become like those who move a boundary line; Upon them I will pour out my wrath like water.
text.DRB: The princes of Juda are become as they that take the bound: I will pour out my wrath as water upon them.

text.NRSV: Ephraim is oppressed, crushed in judgment, because he was determined to go after vanity.
text.Rotherham: Oppressed, is Ephraim, crushed in judgment,—because he hath, wilfully, walked after falsehood.
text.KJV: Ephraim is oppressed and broken in judgment, because he willingly walked after the commandment.
text.NABRE: Ephraim is oppressed, crushed by judgment, for he has willingly gone after filth!
text.DRB: Ephraim is suffering calumny, broken in judgement: because he began to go after filthiness.

text.NRSV: Therefore I am like maggots to Ephraim, and like rottenness to the house of Judah.
text.Rotherham: But, I, was like a moth, to Ephraim,—and like rotten wood to the house of Judah.
text.KJV: Therefore will I be unto Ephraim as a moth, and to the house of Judah as rottenness.
text.NABRE: I am like a moth for Ephraim, like rot for the house of Judah.
text.DRB: And I as it were a moth to Ephraim: & as the rot to the house of Juda.

text.NRSV: When Ephraim saw his sickness, and Judah his wound, then Ephraim went to Assyria, and sent to the great king. But he is not able to cure you or heal your wound.
text.Rotherham: When Ephraim, saw, his injury, and Judah his wound, then went Ephraim unto Assyria, and [Judah] sent unto a hostile king,—yet, he, cannot heal you, nor will the wound, remove from you.
text.KJV: When Ephraim saw his sickness, and Judah saw his wound, then went Ephraim to the Assyrian, and sent to king Jareb: yet could he not heal you, nor cure you of your wound.
text.NABRE: When Ephraim saw his infirmity, and Judah his sore, Ephraim went to Assyria, and sent to the great king. But he cannot heal you, nor take away your sore.
text.DRB: And Ephraim saw his sickness, and Judas his band: and Ephraim went to Assur, and sent to the king revenger: and he shall not be able to heal you, neither shall he be able to loose the band from you.

text.NRSV: For I will be like a lion to Ephraim, and like a young lion to the house of Judah. I myself will tear and go away; I will carry off, and no one shall rescue.
text.Rotherham: For, I, will be as a lion unto Ephraim, and as a young lion to the house of Judah,—I, I, will tear in pieces, and depart, I will carry off, and none be able to rescue.
text.KJV: For I will be unto Ephraim as a lion, and as a young lion to the house of Judah: I, even I, will tear and go away; I will take away, and none shall rescue him.
text.NABRE: For I am like a lion to Ephraim, like a young lion to the house of Judah; It is I who tear the prey and depart, I carry it away and no one can save it.
text.DRB: Because I as it were a lioness to Ephraim, and as a lion's whelp to the house of Juda: I, I will take, and go: I will take away, and there is none that can deliver.

text.NRSV: I will return again to my place until they acknowledge their guilt and seek my face; in their distress they will beg my favor.
text.Rotherham: I will depart, will return unto my place! till what time they acknowledge their guilt, and seek my face,—In their trouble, will they make for me diligent search.
text.KJV: I will go and return to my place, till they acknowledge their offence, and seek my face: in their affliction they will seek me early.
text.NABRE: Insincere Conversion I will go back to my place until they make reparation and seek my presence. In their affliction, they shall look for me.
text.DRB: Going I will return to my place: until you fail and seek my face.

text.NRSV: “Come, let us return to the Lord; for it is he who has torn, and he will heal us; he has struck down, and he will bind us up.
text.Rotherham: Come, and let us return unto Yahweh! for, he, hath torn, that he might heal us,—smitten, that he might bind us up.
text.KJV: Come, and let us return unto the Lord: for he hath torn, and he will heal us; he hath smitten, and he will bind us up.
text.NABRE: “Come, let us return to the Lord, For it is he who has torn, but he will heal us; he has struck down, but he will bind our wounds.
text.DRB: In their tribulation early they will rise up to me: Come, & let us return to our Lord.

text.NRSV: After two days he will revive us; on the third day he will raise us up, that we may live before him.
text.Rotherham: He will bring us to life, after two days,—on the third day, will he raise us up, that we may live before him.
text.KJV: After two days will he revive us: in the third day he will raise us up, and we shall live in his sight.
text.NABRE: He will revive us after two days; on the third day he will raise us up, to live in his presence.
text.DRB: Because he hath wounded, & will heal us: he will strike, & will cure us.

text.NRSV: Let us know, let us press on to know the Lord; his appearing is as sure as the dawn; he will come to us like the showers, like the spring rains that water the earth.”
text.Rotherham: Then let us know—let us press on to know—Yahweh, Like the dawn, is his coming forth assured,—that he may come like a down-pour upon us, like the harvest-rain, [and] the seed-rain of the land.
text.KJV: Then shall we know, if we follow on to know the Lord: his going forth is prepared as the morning; and he shall come unto us as the rain, as the latter and former rain unto the earth.
text.NABRE: Let us know, let us strive to know the Lord; as certain as the dawn is his coming. He will come to us like the rain, like spring rain that waters the earth.”
text.DRB: He will revive us after two days: in the third day he will raise us up, & we shall live in his sight. We shall know, & we shall follow, that we may know our Lord. As the morning light, is his coming forth prepared, and he will come to us as a shower timely, and late to the earth.

text.NRSV: What shall I do with you, O Ephraim? What shall I do with you, O Judah? Your love is like a morning cloud, like the dew that goes away early.
text.Rotherham: What can I do unto thee, O Ephraim? What can I do unto thee, O Judah? for, your lovingkindness, is like a morning cloud, yea, like the dew, early departing!
text.KJV: O Ephraim, what shall I do unto thee? O Judah, what shall I do unto thee? for your goodness is as a morning cloud, and as the early dew it goeth away.
text.NABRE: What can I do with you, Ephraim? What can I do with you, Judah? Your loyalty is like morning mist, like the dew that disappears early.
text.DRB: What shall I do to thee Ephraim? What shall I do to thee Juda? Your mercy as a morning cloud, & as the dew passing away in the morning.

text.NRSV: Therefore I have hewn them by the prophets, I have killed them by the words of my mouth, and my judgment goes forth as the light.
text.Rotherham: For this cause, have I hewn them in pieces by the prophets, I have slain them by the sayings of my mouth,—and, my justice, as a light goeth forth.
text.KJV: Therefore have I hewed them by the prophets; I have slain them by the words of my mouth: and thy judgments are as the light that goeth forth.
text.NABRE: For this reason I struck them down through the prophets, I killed them by the words of my mouth; my judgment shines forth like the light.
text.DRB: For this have I hewed in Prophets, I have killed them in the words of my mouth: and thy judgements shall come forth as the light:

text.NRSV: For I desire steadfast love and not sacrifice, the knowledge of God rather than burnt offerings.
text.Rotherham: For, lovingkindness, I desired, and not sacrifice,—and the knowledge of God, more than ascending-offerings.
text.KJV: For I desired mercy, and not sacrifice; and the knowledge of God more than burnt offerings.
text.NABRE: For it is loyalty that I desire, not sacrifice, and knowledge of God rather than burnt offerings.
text.DRB: because I would mercy, and not sacrifice: and the knowledge of God more than holocausts.

text.NRSV: But at Adam they transgressed the covenant; there they dealt faithlessly with me.
text.Rotherham: But, they, like Adam, have transgressed a covenant,—There, have they dealt treacherously with me.
text.KJV: But they like men have transgressed the covenant: there have they dealt treacherously against me.
text.NABRE: Further Crimes of Israel But they, at Adam, violated the covenant; there they betrayed me.
text.DRB: But they as Adam have transgressed the covenant, there have they prevaricated against me.

text.NRSV: Gilead is a city of evildoers, tracked with blood.
text.Rotherham: Gilead, is a city of workers of iniquity,—tracked with blood.
text.KJV: Gilead is a city of them that work iniquity, and is polluted with blood.
text.NABRE: Gilead is a city of evildoers, tracked with blood.
text.DRB: Galaad a city of them that work idol, supplanted with blood.

text.NRSV: As robbers lie in wait for someone, so the priests are banded together; they murder on the road to Shechem, they commit a monstrous crime.
text.Rotherham: And, like liers in wait for a man, in troops, is a band of priests, on the road, will they murder towards Shechem,—because, a shameful deed, they have done.
text.KJV: And as troops of robbers wait for a man, so the company of priests murder in the way by consent: for they commit lewdness.
text.NABRE: Like brigands lying in wait is the band of priests. They murder on the road to Shechem, indeed they commit a monstrous crime.
text.DRB: And as it were the jaws of men that are robbers, partaker with the Priests, of them that in the way kill those that pass out of Sichem: because they have wrought wickedness.

text.NRSV: In the house of Israel I have seen a horrible thing; Ephraim’s whoredom is there, Israel is defiled.
text.Rotherham: In the house of Israel, have I seen a horrible thing,—there, the unchastity of Ephraim, defiled is Israel.
text.KJV: I have seen an horrible thing in the house of Israel: there is the whoredom of Ephraim, Israel is defiled.
text.NABRE: In the house of Israel I have seen a horrible thing: there is found Ephraim’s prostitution, Israel is defiled.
text.DRB: In the house of Israel I saw an horrible thing: there the fornications of Ephraim: Israel is contaminated.

text.NRSV: For you also, O Judah, a harvest is appointed. When I would restore the fortunes of my people,
text.Rotherham: Judah too! a harvest is appointed for thee,—in that I will bring back the captivity of my people.
text.KJV: Also, O Judah, he hath set an harvest for thee, when I returned the captivity of my people.
text.NABRE: For you also, Judah, a harvest has been appointed!
text.DRB: Yea and thou Juda put thee an harvest, when I shall convert the captivity of my people.

text.NRSV: when I would heal Israel, the corruption of Ephraim is revealed, and the wicked deeds of Samaria; for they deal falsely, the thief breaks in, and the bandits raid outside.
text.Rotherham: When I would have brought healing to Israel, then was disclosed the iniquity of Ephraim, and the wicked doings of Samaria, for they have wrought falsehood,—when, a thief, would enter, a band roamed about, outside,
text.KJV: When I would have healed Israel, then the iniquity of Ephraim was discovered, and the wickedness of Samaria: for they commit falsehood; and the thief cometh in, and the troop of robbers spoileth without.
text.NABRE: Thieves break in, bandits roam outside.
text.DRB: When I would heal Israel, the iniquity of Ephraim was revealed, & the malice of Samaria, because they have wrought lying, & the thief hath entered in spoiling, the robber without.

text.NRSV: But they do not consider that I remember all their wickedness. Now their deeds surround them, they are before my face.
text.Rotherham: And they say not to their own hearts, that, all their wickedness, I remember, now, have their doings, beset them about, right before my face, have they been done.
text.KJV: And they consider not in their hearts that I remember all their wickedness: now their own doings have beset them about; they are before my face.
text.NABRE: Yet they do not call to mind that I remember all their wickedness. Now their crimes surround them, present to my sight.
text.DRB: And lest perhaps they may say in their hearts, that I have remembered all their malice: now have their own inventions compassed them, they have been done before me.

text.NRSV: By their wickedness they make the king glad, and the officials by their treachery.
text.Rotherham: By their wickedness, they gladden a king, and, by their flatteries,—rulers.
text.KJV: They make the king glad with their wickedness, and the princes with their lies.
text.NABRE: Israel’s Domestic Politics With their wickedness they make the king rejoice, the princes too, with their treacherous deeds.
text.DRB: In their malice they have rejoiced the king: and in their lies the princes.

text.NRSV: They are all adulterers; they are like a heated oven, whose baker desists from stirring the fire, from the kneading of the dough until it is leavened.
text.Rotherham: They all, are adulterers, like an oven too hot for the baker,—who leaveth off stoking, after kneading the dough, till the whole be leavened.
text.KJV: They are all adulterers, as an oven heated by the baker, who ceaseth from raising after he hath kneaded the dough, until it be leavened.
text.NABRE: They are all adulterers, like a blazing oven, Which the baker quits stoking, after the dough’s kneading until its rising.
text.DRB: All they committing adultery, as it were an oven heated of the baker: the city was quiet a little from the commistion of leaven, till the whole was leavened.

text.NRSV: On the day of our king the officials became sick with the heat of wine; he stretched out his hand with mockers.
text.Rotherham: In the day of our king, the rulers, have made themselves ill, with the heat of wine,—he hath extended his hand with scoffers.
text.KJV: In the day of our king the princes have made him sick with bottles of wine; he stretched out his hand with scorners.
text.NABRE: On the day of our king, they made the princes sick with poisoned wine; he extended his hand to the scoffers.
text.DRB: The day of our king, the princes began to rage by reason of wine: he stretched out his hand with the scorners.

text.NRSV: For they are kindled like an oven, their heart burns within them; all night their anger smolders; in the morning it blazes like a flaming fire.
text.Rotherham: For they have made ready, like an oven, their heart, by their lying in wait,—all the night, their baker sleepeth, in the morning, he, kindleth up as it were a blazing fire.
text.KJV: For they have made ready their heart like an oven, whiles they lie in wait: their baker sleepeth all the night; in the morning it burneth as a flaming fire.
text.NABRE: For they draw near in ambush with their hearts like an oven. All the night their anger sleeps; in the morning it flares like a blazing fire.
text.DRB: Because they have applied their heart as an oven, when he lay in wait for them: he slept all the night baking them, in the morning himself heated as a fire of flame.

text.NRSV: All of them are hot as an oven, and they devour their rulers. All their kings have fallen; none of them calls upon me.
text.Rotherham: They all, become hot as an oven, and devour their judges,—all their kings, have fallen, there hath been none among them crying unto me.
text.KJV: They are all hot as an oven, and have devoured their judges; all their kings are fallen: there is none among them that calleth unto me.
text.NABRE: They are all heated like ovens, and consume their rulers. All their kings have fallen; none of them calls upon me.
text.DRB: All were heated as an oven, and have devoured their judges: all their kings are fallen: there is none amongst them that cryeth unto me.

text.NRSV: Ephraim mixes himself with the peoples; Ephraim is a cake not turned.
text.Rotherham: As for Ephraim! with the peoples, hath he been mingling himself,—Ephraim, is a cake not turned.
text.KJV: Ephraim, he hath mixed himself among the people; Ephraim is a cake not turned.
text.NABRE: Israel’s Foreign Politics Ephraim is mixed with the nations, Ephraim is an unturned cake.
text.DRB: Ephraim himself was mingled in the peoples: Ephraim is become as hearth-baken-bread that is not turned.

text.NRSV: Foreigners devour his strength, but he does not know it; gray hairs are sprinkled upon him, but he does not know it.
text.Rotherham: Foreigners have, eaten up, his strength, and, he, knoweth it not,—even gray hairs, are sprinkled upon him, and, he, knoweth it not.
text.KJV: Strangers have devoured his strength, and he knoweth it not: yea, gray hairs are here and there upon him, yet he knoweth not.
text.NABRE: Strangers have consumed his strength, but he does not know it; Gray hairs are strewn on his head, but he takes no notice of it.
text.DRB: Strangers have eaten his strength, & he knew not: yea hoar hairs also were poured out on him, & he was ignorant.

text.NRSV: Israel’s pride testifies against him; yet they do not return to the Lord their God, or seek him, for all this.
text.Rotherham: Therefore doth the Excellency of Israel, answer, to his face; yet have they not returned unto Yahweh their God, nor have they sought him, in spite of all this!
text.KJV: And the pride of Israel testifieth to his face: and they do not return to the Lord their God, nor seek him for all this.
text.NABRE: The arrogance of Israel bears witness against him; yet they do not return to the Lord, their God, nor seek him, despite all this.
text.DRB: And the pride of Israel shall be humbled in his face: neither did they return to the Lord their God, & they have not sought him in all these.

text.NRSV: Ephraim has become like a dove, silly and without sense; they call upon Egypt, they go to Assyria.
text.Rotherham: So then, Ephraim, hath become, like a simple dove, having no understanding, on Egypt, have they called, to Assyria, have they gone,
text.KJV: Ephraim also is like a silly dove without heart: they call to Egypt, they go to Assyria.
text.NABRE: Ephraim is like a dove, silly and senseless; They call upon Egypt, they go to Assyria.
text.DRB: And Ephraim is become as a dove seduced, not having an heart: they invocated Ægypt, they went to the Assyrians.

text.NRSV: As they go, I will cast my net over them; I will bring them down like birds of the air; I will discipline them according to the report made to their assembly.
text.Rotherham: Whithersoever they go, I will spread over them my net, like a bird of the heavens, will I bring them down, I will chastise them, by the time the report can reach the flock of them.
text.KJV: When they shall go, I will spread my net upon them; I will bring them down as the fowls of the heaven; I will chastise them, as their congregation hath heard.
text.NABRE: When they go I will spread my net around them, like birds in the air I will bring them down. I will chastise them when I hear of their assembly.
text.DRB: And when they shall go forth, I will spread my net upon them: as a fowl of the heaven will I pluck them down, I will beat them according to the hearing of their assembly.

text.NRSV: Woe to them, for they have strayed from me! Destruction to them, for they have rebelled against me! I would redeem them, but they speak lies against me.
text.Rotherham: Woe to them! for they have taken flight from me, destruction to them! for they have transgressed against me,—when, I, would have ransomed them, then, they, spake—concerning me—falsehoods.
text.KJV: Woe unto them! for they have fled from me: destruction unto them! because they have transgressed against me: though I have redeemed them, yet they have spoken lies against me.
text.NABRE: Woe to them, for they have strayed from me! Ruin to them, for they have rebelled against me! Though I wished to redeem them, they spoke lies against me.
text.DRB: Woe to them, because they have revolted from me: they shall be wasted because they have prevaricated against me: & I redeemed them & they have spoken lies against me.

text.NRSV: They do not cry to me from the heart, but they wail upon their beds; for grain and wine they gash themselves, they rebel against me.
text.Rotherham: Neither made they outcry unto me, in their heart, although they kept on howling upon their beds, over corn and new wine, they gathered themselves together, they rebelled against me.
text.KJV: And they have not cried unto me with their heart, when they howled upon their beds: they assemble themselves for corn and wine, and they rebel against me.
text.NABRE: They have not cried to me from their hearts when they wailed upon their beds; For wheat and wine they lacerated themselves; they rebelled against me.
text.DRB: And they have not cried to me in their heart, but they howled in their chambers: upon wheat and wine they chewed the cud, they are revolted from me.

text.NRSV: It was I who trained and strengthened their arms, yet they plot evil against me.
text.Rotherham: When, I, had warned them, I strengthened their arm,—yet, against me, kept they on devising wickedness.
text.KJV: Though I have bound and strengthened their arms, yet do they imagine mischief against me.
text.NABRE: Though I trained and strengthened their arms, yet they devised evil against me.
text.DRB: And I have taught them, and strengthened their arms: and against me they have thought malice.

text.NRSV: They turn to that which does not profit; they have become like a treacherous bow; their officials shall fall by the sword because of the rage of their tongue. This shall be their derision in the land of Egypt.
text.Rotherham: They would return—not to him who is on high! They have become like a deceitful bow, their rulers, shall fall by the sword, for the rage of their tongue, this, [shall be] their derision in the land of Egypt.
text.KJV: They return, but not to the most High: they are like a deceitful bow: their princes shall fall by the sword for the rage of their tongue: this shall be their derision in the land of Egypt.
text.NABRE: They have again become useless, they have been like a treacherous bow. Their princes shall fall by the sword because of the insolence of their tongues; thus they shall be mocked in the land of Egypt.
text.DRB: They returned, that they might be without yoke: they became as a deceitful bow: their princes shall fall by the sword, for the fury of their tongue. This is their scorning in the Land of Ægypt.

text.NRSV: Set the trumpet to your lips! One like a vulture is over the house of the Lord, because they have broken my covenant, and transgressed my law.
text.Rotherham: To thy mouth, with a horn! Like an eagle, on the house of Yahweh,—because they have violated my covenant, and, against my law, have they transgressed.
text.KJV: Set the trumpet to thy mouth. He shall come as an eagle against the house of the Lord, because they have transgressed my covenant, and trespassed against my law.
text.NABRE: Put the trumpet to your lips! One like an eagle is over the house of the Lord! Because they have violated my covenant, and rebelled against my law,
text.DRB: In thy throat let there be a trumpet as an eagle upon the house of the Lord: for that they have transgressed my covenant, and have prevaricated my law.

text.NRSV: Israel cries to me, “My God, we know you!”
text.Rotherham: Unto me, shall they make outcry, My God! we acknowledge thee—[we], Israel!
text.KJV: Israel shall cry unto me, My God, we know thee.
text.NABRE: They cry out to me, “My God! We know you!”
text.DRB: Me they shall invocate: My God, we Israel have known thee.

text.NRSV: Israel has spurned the good; the enemy shall pursue him.
text.Rotherham: Israel hath cast away what is good,—an enemy, shall pursue him.
text.KJV: Israel hath cast off the thing that is good: the enemy shall pursue him.
text.NABRE: But Israel has rejected what is good; the enemy shall pursue him.
text.DRB: Israel hath cast away the good thing, the enemy shall persecute him.

text.NRSV: They made kings, but not through me; they set up princes, but without my knowledge. With their silver and gold they made idols for their own destruction.
text.Rotherham: They, have appointed kings, but not from me, have made rulers, but I have not acknowledged them: of their silver and their gold, they made themselves idols, to the end they might be cut off.
text.KJV: They have set up kings, but not by me: they have made princes, and I knew it not: of their silver and their gold have they made them idols, that they may be cut off.
text.NABRE: They made kings, but not by my authority; they established princes, but without my knowledge. With their silver and gold they made idols for themselves, to their own destruction.
text.DRB: They have reigned, & not of me: they have been Princes, & I knew not: their silver & their gold they made idols to themselves, that they might perish.

text.NRSV: Your calf is rejected, O Samaria. My anger burns against them. How long will they be incapable of innocence?
text.Rotherham: He hath cast away thy calf, O Samaria, kindled is mine anger upon them,—How long shall they not endure to be innocent?
text.KJV: Thy calf, O Samaria, hath cast thee off; mine anger is kindled against them: how long will it be ere they attain to innocency?
text.NABRE: He has rejected your calf, Samaria! My wrath is kindled against them; How long will they be incapable of innocence in Israel?
text.DRB: Thy calf is cast off o Samaria, my fury is wrath against them. How long can they not be cleansed?

text.NRSV: For it is from Israel, an artisan made it; it is not God. The calf of Samaria shall be broken to pieces.
text.Rotherham: For, of Israel, is even that thing! A craftsman, made it, and, a No-god, it is! For, into fragments, shall the Calf of Samaria be broken.
text.KJV: For from Israel was it also: the workman made it; therefore it is not God: but the calf of Samaria shall be broken in pieces.
text.NABRE: An artisan made it, it is no god at all. The calf of Samaria will be dashed to pieces.
text.DRB: Because it-self also is of Israel: the workman made it, and it is not God: because the calf of Samaria shall be as spiders' webs.

text.NRSV: For they sow the wind, and they shall reap the whirlwind. The standing grain has no heads, it shall yield no meal; if it were to yield, foreigners would devour it.
text.Rotherham: For, to the wind, they sow, and, to the whirlwind, they reap: stalk, hath it none, That which shooteth forth, shall yield no meal, If so be it yield, foreigners, swallow it lip,
text.KJV: For they have sown the wind, and they shall reap the whirlwind: it hath no stalk: the bud shall yield no meal: if so be it yield, the strangers shall swallow it up.
text.NABRE: When they sow the wind, they will reap the whirlwind; The stalk of grain that forms no head can yield no flour; Even if it could, strangers would swallow it.
text.DRB: Because they shall sow wind, & reap a whirlwind: there is no standing stalk in it, the bud shall not yield meal: & if it do yield, strangers shall eat it.

text.NRSV: Israel is swallowed up; now they are among the nations as a useless vessel.
text.Rotherham: Swallowed up, is Israel; Now, have they gone among the nations, like a vessel in which no man taketh, delight.
text.KJV: Israel is swallowed up: now shall they be among the Gentiles as a vessel wherein is no pleasure.
text.NABRE: Israel is swallowed up; now they are among the nations, like a useless vessel.
text.DRB: Israel is devoured: Now is he become in nations as an unclean vessel.
