text.NRSV: For they have gone up to Assyria, a wild ass wandering alone; Ephraim has hired lovers.
text.Rotherham: For, they, have gone up to Assyria, A wild ass going alone for himself, is Ephraim! They have hired lovers!
text.KJV: For they are gone up to Assyria, a wild ass alone by himself: Ephraim hath hired lovers.
text.NABRE: For they went up to Assyria— a wild ass off on its own— Ephraim bargained for lovers.
text.DRB: Because they went up to Assur, a wild ass solitary to himself: Ephraim have given gifts to lovers.

text.NRSV: Though they hire allies among the nations, I will soon gather them up; and they shall soon begin to languish under the burden of kings and princes.
text.Rotherham: Even though they hire them among the nations, at once, will I gather them, when they have begun to be diminished by reason of the burden of the king of rulers.
text.KJV: Yea, though they have hired among the nations, now will I gather them, and they shall sorrow a little for the burden of the king of princes.
text.NABRE: Even though they bargain with the nations, I will now gather them together; They will soon succumb under the burden of king and princes.
text.DRB: Yea and when they shall hire the nations with reward, now will I gather them together: & they shall rest a while from the burden of the king and the princes.

text.NRSV: When Ephraim multiplied altars to expiate sin, they became to him altars for sinning.
text.Rotherham: Because Ephraim hath multiplied altars sinfully, they have become to him the altars of sin.
text.KJV: Because Ephraim hath made many altars to sin, altars shall be unto him to sin.
text.NABRE: When Ephraim made many altars to expiate sin, they became altars for sinning.
text.DRB: Because Ephraim hath multiplied altars to sin: altars are made to him unto sin.

text.NRSV: Though I write for him the multitude of my instructions, they are regarded as a strange thing.
text.Rotherham: I have been wont to write for him the myriad things of my law,—Like something alien, have they been accounted.
text.KJV: I have written to him the great things of my law, but they were counted as a strange thing.
text.NABRE: Though I write for him my many instructions, they are considered like a stranger’s.
text.DRB: I will write to him my manifold laws, which have been accounted as strange.

text.NRSV: Though they offer choice sacrifices, though they eat flesh, the Lord does not accept them. Now he will remember their iniquity, and punish their sins; they shall return to Egypt.
text.Rotherham: My sacrificial gifts, have they been sacrificing as [common] flesh, and have eaten, Yahweh, hath not accepted them,—Now, will he call to mind their iniquity, that he may punish their sin, they, to Egypt, will return.
text.KJV: They sacrifice flesh for the sacrifices of mine offerings, and eat it; but the Lord accepteth them not; now will he remember their iniquity, and visit their sins: they shall return to Egypt.
text.NABRE: They love sacrifice, they sacrifice meat and eat it, but the Lord is not pleased with them. Now he will remember their guilt and punish their sins; they shall return to Egypt.
text.DRB: They shall offer hosts, they shall immolate flesh, and shall eat, and the Lord will not receive them: now will he remember their iniquity, and will visit their sins: they shall return into Ægypt.

text.NRSV: For Israel has forgotten his Maker, and built palaces; and Judah has multiplied fortified cities; but I will send a fire upon his cities, and it shall devour his strongholds.
text.Rotherham: And so Israel hath forgotten his Maker, and hath built temples, and, Judah, hath multiplied fortified cities,—Therefore will I send a fire upon his cities, and it shall consume the palaces thereof.
text.KJV: For Israel hath forgotten his Maker, and buildeth temples; and Judah hath multiplied fenced cities: but I will send a fire upon his cities, and it shall devour the palaces thereof.
text.NABRE: Israel has forgotten his maker and has built palaces. Judah, too, has fortified many cities, but I will send fire upon his cities, to devour their strongholds.
text.DRB: And Israel hath forgotten his maker, and hath built temples: and Judas hath multiplied fenced cities: & I will cast fire on his cities, and it shall devour the houses thereof.

text.NRSV: Do not rejoice, O Israel! Do not exult as other nations do; for you have played the whore, departing from your God. You have loved a prostitute’s pay on all threshing floors.
text.Rotherham: Do not rejoice, O Israel, with exultation, like the peoples, for thou hast gone away unchastely from beside thy God,—thou hast loved a present, upon all the threshing-floors of corn!
text.KJV: Rejoice not, O Israel, for joy, as other people: for thou hast gone a whoring from thy God, thou hast loved a reward upon every cornfloor.
text.NABRE: Do not rejoice, Israel, do not exult like the nations! For you have prostituted yourself, abandoning your God, loving a prostitute’s fee upon every threshing floor.
text.DRB: Be not glad Israel, rejoice not as the peoples: because thou hast fornicated from thy God, thou hast loved reward above all the floors of wheat.

text.NRSV: Threshing floor and wine press shall not feed them, and the new wine shall fail them.
text.Rotherham: Threshing-floor and wine-vat, will not feed them, and, new wine, will deny them.
text.KJV: The floor and the winepress shall not feed them, and the new wine shall fail in her.
text.NABRE: Threshing floor and wine press will not nourish them, the new wine will fail them.
text.DRB: The floor and the winepress shall not feed them, and the wine shall lie to them.

text.NRSV: They shall not remain in the land of the Lord; but Ephraim shall return to Egypt, and in Assyria they shall eat unclean food.
text.Rotherham: They shall not dwell in the land of Yahweh, but Ephraim shall return to Egypt, and, in Assyria—that which is unclean, shall they eat.
text.KJV: They shall not dwell in the Lord’s land; but Ephraim shall return to Egypt, and they shall eat unclean things in Assyria.
text.NABRE: They will not dwell in the Lord ’s land; Ephraim will return to Egypt, and in Assyria they will eat unclean food.
text.DRB: They shall not dwell in the land of our Lord: Ephraim is returned into Ægypt, and among the Assyrians he hath eaten the thing polluted.

text.NRSV: They shall not pour drink offerings of wine to the Lord, and their sacrifices shall not please him. Such sacrifices shall be like mourners’ bread; all who eat of it shall be defiled; for their bread shall be for their hunger only; it shall not come into the house of the Lord.
text.Rotherham: They shall not pour out to Yahweh—wine, neither shall they be pleasing to him, their sacrifices, are as the food of mourning to them, all that eat thereof, shall defile themselves, because, their food for their appetite, entereth not into the house of Yahweh.
text.KJV: They shall not offer wine offerings to the Lord, neither shall they be pleasing unto him: their sacrifices shall be unto them as the bread of mourners; all that eat thereof shall be polluted: for their bread for their soul shall not come into the house of the Lord.
text.NABRE: They will not pour libations of wine to the Lord, and their sacrifices will not please him. Their bread will be like mourners’ bread, that makes unclean all who eat of it; Their food will be for their own appetites; it cannot enter the house of the Lord.
text.DRB: They shall not offer wine to our Lord, and they shall not please him: their sacrifices as the bread of mourners: all that shall eat it shall be contaminated: because their bread for their soul, shall not enter into the house of our Lord.

text.NRSV: What will you do on the day of appointed festival, and on the day of the feast of the Lord?
text.Rotherham: What will ye do, for the day of appointed meeting? and in the day of the festival of Yahweh?
text.KJV: What will ye do in the solemn day, and in the day of the feast of the Lord?
text.NABRE: What will you do on the festival day, the day of the Lord ’s feast?
text.DRB: What will you do in the solemn day, in the day of the festivity of our Lord?

text.NRSV: For even if they escape destruction, Egypt shall gather them, Memphis shall bury them. Nettles shall possess their precious things of silver; thorns shall be in their tents.
text.Rotherham: For, though they have gone from destruction, yet, Egypt, shall gather them, Memphis, shall bury them,—as for their silver favorites! thistles, shall possess them, thorns in their tents.
text.KJV: For, lo, they are gone because of destruction: Egypt shall gather them up, Memphis shall bury them: the pleasant places for their silver, nettles shall possess them: thorns shall be in their tabernacles.
text.NABRE: When they flee from the devastation, Egypt will gather them, Memphis will bury them. Weeds will overgrow their silver treasures, and thorns, their tents.
text.DRB: For behold they are gone forth from destruction: Ægypt shall gather them together, Memphis shall bury them: their silver to be desired the nettle shall inherit, the bur in their tabernacles.

text.NRSV: The days of punishment have come, the days of recompense have come; Israel shall know it. The prophet is a fool, the man of the spirit is mad, because of your great iniquity and great enmity.
text.Rotherham: Come are the days of visitation, come are the days of recompense, Let Israel know! The prophet is foolish, the man of the spirit doth rave, because of the greatness of thine iniquity, therefore great is the prosecution.
text.KJV: The days of visitation are come, the days of recompence are come; Israel shall know it: the prophet is a fool, the spiritual man is mad, for the multitude of thine iniquity, and the great hatred.
text.NABRE: They have come, the days of punishment! they have come, the days of recompense! Let Israel know it! “The prophet is a fool, the man of the spirit is mad!” Because your iniquity is great, great, too, is your hostility.
text.DRB: The days of visitation are come, the days are come of retributions: know o Israel the foolish Prophet, the mad spiritual man, for the multitude of thine iniquity, the multitude also madness.

text.NRSV: The prophet is a sentinel for Ephraim with my God, yet a fowler’s snare is on all his ways, and enmity in the house of his God.
text.Rotherham: The watchman of Ephraim, [should have been] with my God: As for the prophet, the snare of the fowler, is on all his ways, a prosecution [awaiteth him], in the house of his God.
text.KJV: The watchman of Ephraim was with my God: but the prophet is a snare of a fowler in all his ways, and hatred in the house of his God.
text.NABRE: The watchman of Ephraim, the people of my God, is the prophet; yet a fowler’s snare is on all his ways, hostility in the house of his God.
text.DRB: The watchman of Ephraim with my God: the Prophet is made a snare of ruin upon all his ways, madness in the house of his God.

text.NRSV: They have deeply corrupted themselves as in the days of Gibeah; he will remember their iniquity, he will punish their sins.
text.Rotherham: They have deeply corrupted themselves, like the days of Gibeah: he will call to mind their iniquity, he will punish their sins.
text.KJV: They have deeply corrupted themselves, as in the days of Gibeah: therefore he will remember their iniquity, he will visit their sins.
text.NABRE: They have sunk to the depths of corruption, as in the days of Gibeah; God will remember their iniquity and punish their sins.
text.DRB: They have sinned deeply, as in the days of Gabaa: he will remember their iniquity, and will visit their sin.

text.NRSV: Like grapes in the wilderness, I found Israel. Like the first fruit on the fig tree, in its first season, I saw your ancestors. But they came to Baal-peor, and consecrated themselves to a thing of shame, and became detestable like the thing they loved.
text.Rotherham: Like grapes in the desert, found I Israel, like the first-ripe in the fig-tree when it is young, saw I your fathers,—they, entered Baal-peor, and devoted themselves to the Shameful Thing, Then became their abominations like their lusts.
text.KJV: I found Israel like grapes in the wilderness; I saw your fathers as the firstripe in the fig tree at her first time: but they went to Baal–peor, and separated themselves unto that shame; and their abominations were according as they loved.
text.NABRE: From Former Glory to a History of Corruption Like grapes in the desert, I found Israel; Like the first fruits of the fig tree, its first to ripen, I looked on your ancestors. But when they came to Baal-peor and consecrated themselves to the Shameful One, they became as abhorrent as the thing they loved.
text.DRB: As grapes in the desert I found Israel: as the first fruits of the fig-tree in the top thereof I saw their fathers: but they have entered into Beelphegor, and are alienated into confusion, and are become abominable, as those things, which they loved.

text.NRSV: Ephraim’s glory shall fly away like a bird—no birth, no pregnancy, no conception!
text.Rotherham: As for Ephraim! like a bird, did their glory, fly away,—no birth, and none with child, no conception.
text.KJV: As for Ephraim, their glory shall fly away like a bird, from the birth, and from the womb, and from the conception.
text.NABRE: Ephraim is like a bird: their glory flies away— no birth, no pregnancy, no conception.
text.DRB: Ephraim as a bird hath flown away, their glory from birth, and from the womb, and from conception.

text.NRSV: Even if they bring up children, I will bereave them until no one is left. Woe to them indeed when I depart from them!
text.Rotherham: Yea, though they rear their children, yet will I make them childless, till there be no human being,—for it is, nothing less than woe, to them when I depart from them!
text.KJV: Though they bring up their children, yet will I bereave them, that there shall not be a man left: yea, woe also to them when I depart from them!
text.NABRE: Even though they bring up their children, I will make them childless, until no one is left. Indeed, woe to them when I turn away from them!
text.DRB: But if they shall nourish up their children, I will make them without children among men: yea and woe to them, when I shall depart from them.

text.NRSV: Once I saw Ephraim as a young palm planted in a lovely meadow, but now Ephraim must lead out his children for slaughter.
text.Rotherham: Ephraim! just as I provided for Tyre, was planted in a meadow,—yet, Ephraim, must needs bring forth for a murderer his children.
text.KJV: Ephraim, as I saw Tyrus, is planted in a pleasant place: but Ephraim shall bring forth his children to the murderer.
text.NABRE: Ephraim, as I saw, was a tree planted in a meadow; But now Ephraim will bring out his children to the slaughterer!
text.DRB: Ephraim, as I saw, was Tyre founded in beauty: & Ephraim shall lead out his children to the murderer.

text.NRSV: Give them, O Lord—what will you give? Give them a miscarrying womb and dry breasts.
text.Rotherham: Give them, O Yahweh—what wilt thou give? Give them, a miscarrying womb, and breasts dried up.
text.KJV: Give them, O Lord: what wilt thou give? give them a miscarrying womb and dry breasts.
text.NABRE: Give them, Lord! give them what? Give them a miscarrying womb, and dry breasts!
text.DRB: Give them o Lord, what wilt thou give to them? Give them a womb without children, & dry breasts.

text.NRSV: Every evil of theirs began at Gilgal; there I began to hate them. Because of the wickedness of their deeds I will drive them out of my house. I will love them no more; all their officials are rebels.
text.Rotherham: All their wickedness, is in Gilgal, yea, there, have I come to hate them, For the wickedness of their doings—out of my house, will I drive them forth,—no more will I love them, all their rulers, are unruly.
text.KJV: All their wickedness is in Gilgal: for there I hated them: for the wickedness of their doings I will drive them out of mine house, I will love them no more: all their princes are revolters.
text.NABRE: All their misfortune began in Gilgal; yes, there I rejected them. Because of their wicked deeds I will drive them out of my house. I will love them no longer; all their princes are rebels.
text.DRB: All their wicked in Galgal, because there I hated them: for the malice of their inventions I will cast them forth out of my house: I will not add to love them, all their princes revolters.

text.NRSV: Ephraim is stricken, their root is dried up, they shall bear no fruit. Even though they give birth, I will kill the cherished offspring of their womb.
text.Rotherham: Smitten is Ephraim, their root, hath dried up, fruit, shall they not bear,—yea, though they do bring forth, yet will I slay the darlings of their womb.
text.KJV: Ephraim is smitten, their root is dried up, they shall bear no fruit: yea, though they bring forth, yet will I slay even the beloved fruit of their womb.
text.NABRE: Ephraim is stricken, their root is dried up; they will bear no fruit. Were they to bear children, I would slay the beloved of their womb.
text.DRB: Ephraim is strucken, their root is dried up, they shall yield no fruit. But and if they shall have issue, I will kill the best beloved things of their womb.

text.NRSV: My God will reject them because they have not listened to him; they shall be wanderers among the nations.
text.Rotherham: My God will reject them, because they hearkened not unto him,—that they may become wanderers throughout the nations.
text.KJV: My God will cast them away, because they did not hearken unto him: and they shall be wanderers among the nations.
text.NABRE: My God will disown them because they have not listened to him; they will be wanderers among the nations.
text.DRB: My God will cast them away, because they hear him not: and they shall be vagabonds in the nations.

text.NRSV: Israel is a luxuriant vine that yields its fruit. The more his fruit increased the more altars he built; as his land improved he erected more beautiful pillars.
text.Rotherham: A luxuriant vine, is Israel, fruit, beseemeth him,—According to the abundance of his fruit, hath he brought abundance to the altars, according to the goodliness of his land, hath he made goodly statues.
text.KJV: Israel is an empty vine, he bringeth forth fruit unto himself: according to the multitude of his fruit he hath increased the altars; according to the goodness of his land they have made goodly images.
text.NABRE: The more abundant his fruit, the more altars he built; The more productive his land, the more sacred pillars he set up.
text.DRB: Israel a vine thick of branches, the fruit is made equal to it: according to the multitude of his fruit he hath multiplied altars, according to the plenty of his land he hath abounded in idols.

text.NRSV: Their heart is false; now they must bear their guilt. The Lord will break down their altars, and destroy their pillars.
text.Rotherham: Hypocritical is their heart, Now, shall they be held guilty,—He, will break down their altars, he will destroy their statutes.
text.KJV: Their heart is divided; now shall they be found faulty: he shall break down their altars, he shall spoil their images.
text.NABRE: Their heart is false! Now they will pay for their guilt: God will break down their altars and destroy their sacred pillars.
text.DRB: Their heart is divided, now they shall perish: he shall break their idols, he shall destroy their altars.

text.NRSV: For now they will say: “We have no king, for we do not fear the Lord, and a king—what could he do for us?”
text.Rotherham: For, now, will they say, We have no king,—for we revere not Yahweh, and what could, a king, do for us?
text.KJV: For now they shall say, We have no king, because we feared not the Lord; what then should a king do to us?
text.NABRE: For now they will say, “We have no king! Since we do not fear the Lord, the king—what could he do for us?”
text.DRB: Because they will now say: We have no king: for we fear not our Lord: and what shall a king do to us?

text.NRSV: They utter mere words; with empty oaths they make covenants; so litigation springs up like poisonous weeds in the furrows of the field.
text.Rotherham: They have spoken words, swearing falsely, in solemnizing a covenant,—therefore shall judgment, spring up like a poisonous plant, on the ridges of the field.
text.KJV: They have spoken words, swearing falsely in making a covenant: thus judgment springeth up as hemlock in the furrows of the field.
text.NABRE: They make promises, swear false oaths, and make covenants, While lawsuits sprout like poisonous weeds in the furrows of a field!
text.DRB: You speak words of unprofitable vision, and you shall make a covenant: and judgement shall spring as bitterness upon the furrows of the field.

text.NRSV: The inhabitants of Samaria tremble for the calf of Beth-aven. Its people shall mourn for it, and its idolatrous priests shall wail over it, over its glory that has departed from it.
text.Rotherham: About the calves of Beth-aven, will the inhabitant of Samaria be concerned,—for the people thereof, have mourned over it, and, the ascetics thereof, who, over it, used to exult, [shall mourn] for the glory thereof, because it hath departed therefrom.
text.KJV: The inhabitants of Samaria shall fear because of the calves of Beth–aven: for the people thereof shall mourn over it, and the priests thereof that rejoiced on it, for the glory thereof, because it is departed from it.
text.NABRE: The inhabitants of Samaria are afraid for the calf of Beth-aven; Its people mourn for it and its idolatrous priests wail over it, —over its glory which has departed from it.
text.DRB: The kine of Bethaven have the inhabitants of Samaria worshipped: Because his people mourned upon him, and his temple wardens rejoiced upon him in his glory, because it departed from him.

text.NRSV: The thing itself shall be carried to Assyria as tribute to the great king. Ephraim shall be put to shame, and Israel shall be ashamed of his idol.
text.Rotherham: Itself also, to Assyria, shall be borne along, as a present to a hostile king,—shame, shall Ephraim receive, that Israel, may be ashamed, of his own counsel.
text.KJV: It shall be also carried unto Assyria for a present to king Jareb: Ephraim shall receive shame, and Israel shall be ashamed of his own counsel.
text.NABRE: It too will be carried to Assyria, as an offering to the great king. Ephraim will be put to shame, Israel will be shamed by his schemes.
text.DRB: For he also was carried unto Assur, a gift to the king Revenger: confusion shall take Ephraim, and Israel shall be confused in his own will.

text.NRSV: Samaria’s king shall perish like a chip on the face of the waters.
text.Rotherham: Silenced is Samaria: her king, is as a chip on the face of the waters,
text.KJV: As for Samaria, her king is cut off as the foam upon the water.
text.NABRE: Samaria and her king will disappear, like a twig upon the waters.
text.DRB: Samaria hath made her king to pass as froth upon the face of water.

text.NRSV: The high places of Aven, the sin of Israel, shall be destroyed. Thorn and thistle shall grow up on their altars. They shall say to the mountains, “Cover us,” and to the hills, “Fall on us.”
text.Rotherham: So shall the high places of Aven, be destroyed, the sin of Israel, Thorn and prickle, shall come up on their altars,—Therefore shall they say to the mountains, Cover us, and to the hills, Fall on us.
text.KJV: The high places also of Aven, the sin of Israel, shall be destroyed: the thorn and the thistle shall come up on their altars; and they shall say to the mountains, Cover us; and to the hills, Fall on us.
text.NABRE: The high places of Aven will be destroyed, the sin of Israel; thorns and thistles will overgrow their altars. Then they will cry out to the mountains, “Cover us!” and to the hills, “Fall upon us!”
text.DRB: And the excelses of the idol the sin of Israel shall be destroyed: the bur and the thistle shall grow up over their altars: and they shall say to the mountains: Cover us, & to the little hills: Fall upon us.

text.NRSV: Since the days of Gibeah you have sinned, O Israel; there they have continued. Shall not war overtake them in Gibeah?
text.Rotherham: Beyond the days of Gibeah, hast thou sinned, O Israel: there, came they to a stand, the battle against the sons of perversity, touched them not in Gibeah.
text.KJV: O Israel, thou hast sinned from the days of Gibeah: there they stood: the battle in Gibeah against the children of iniquity did not overtake them.
text.NABRE: War Because of Israel’s Wickedness Since the days of Gibeah you have sinned, Israel. There they took their stand; will war not reach them in Gibeah? Against a perverse people
text.DRB: From the Days of Gabaa, Israel hath sinned, there they stood: the battle in Gabaa upon the children of iniquity shall not apprehend them.

text.NRSV: I will come against the wayward people to punish them; and nations shall be gathered against them when they are punished for their double iniquity.
text.Rotherham: When I please, then will I chastise them,—and there shall be gathered together against them—peoples, they being harnessed to their two Iniquities;
text.KJV: It is in my desire that I should chastise them; and the people shall be gathered against them, when they shall bind themselves in their two furrows.
text.NABRE: I came and I chastised them; Peoples will be gathered against them when I bind them to their two crimes.
text.DRB: According to my desire I will chastise them: & the peoples shall be gathered together upon them, when they shall be chastised for their two iniquities.

text.NRSV: Ephraim was a trained heifer that loved to thresh, and I spared her fair neck; but I will make Ephraim break the ground; Judah must plow; Jacob must harrow for himself.
text.Rotherham: But, Ephraim, shall be a heifer broken in, loving to tread out corn, when, I, have passed over upon her fair neck,—I will drive Ephraim, Judah, shall plow, Jacob, shall harrow to him.
text.KJV: And Ephraim is as an heifer that is taught, and loveth to tread out the corn; but I passed over upon her fair neck: I will make Ephraim to ride; Judah shall plow, and Jacob shall break his clods.
text.NABRE: Ephraim was a trained heifer, that loved to thresh; I myself laid a yoke upon her beautiful neck; I will make Ephraim break ground, Judah must plow, Jacob must harrow for himself:
text.DRB: Ephraim an heifer taught to love threshing, & I have passed over the beauty of her neck: I will ascend upon Ephraim, Judas shall plough, Jacob shall break the furrows to himself.

text.NRSV: Sow for yourselves righteousness; reap steadfast love; break up your fallow ground; for it is time to seek the Lord, that he may come and rain righteousness upon you.
text.Rotherham: Sow to yourselves in righteousness, reap ye at the bidding of lovingkindness, furrow to yourselves the newly-ploughed soil,—then will be the time to seek Yahweh, until he come, that he may rain down righteousness for you.
text.KJV: Sow to yourselves in righteousness, reap in mercy; break up your fallow ground: for it is time to seek the Lord, till he come and rain righteousness upon you.
text.NABRE: “Sow for yourselves justice, reap the reward of loyalty; Break up for yourselves a new field, for it is time to seek the Lord, till he comes and rains justice upon you.”
text.DRB: Sow to yourselves in justice, and reap in the mouth of mercy, fallow ground: but the time to seek our Lord, when he shall come that shall teach you justice.

text.NRSV: You have plowed wickedness, you have reaped injustice, you have eaten the fruit of lies. Because you have trusted in your power and in the multitude of your warriors,
text.Rotherham: Ye have plowed lawlessness, perversity, have ye reaped, ye have eaten the fruit of deception,—because thou didst trust in thy chariots, in the multitude of thy mighty men.
text.KJV: Ye have plowed wickedness, ye have reaped iniquity; ye have eaten the fruit of lies: because thou didst trust in thy way, in the multitude of thy mighty men.
text.NABRE: But you have plowed wickedness, reaped perversity, and eaten the fruit of falsehood. Because you have trusted in your own power, and in your many warriors,
text.DRB: You have ploughed impiety, you have reaped iniquity, you have eaten the fruit of lying: because thou hast trusted in thy ways, in the multitude of thy strong ones.

text.NRSV: therefore the tumult of war shall rise against your people, and all your fortresses shall be destroyed, as Shalman destroyed Beth-arbel on the day of battle when mothers were dashed in pieces with their children.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall there arise a tumult among thy peoples, and, all thy fortresses, shall be plundered, as Shalman plundered Beth-arbel, in the day of battle,—the mother, upon her children, dashed to the ground.
text.KJV: Therefore shall a tumult arise among thy people, and all thy fortresses shall be spoiled, as Shalman spoiled Beth–arbel in the day of battle: the mother was dashed in pieces upon her children.
text.NABRE: The clamor of war shall break out among your people and all your fortresses shall be ravaged As Salman ravaged Beth-arbel on the day of war, smashing mothers along with their children.
text.DRB: Atumult shall arise in thy people: and all thy munitions shall be destroyed as Salmana was destroyed by his house, that took vengeance on Baal in the day of battle, the mother being dashed in pieces upon the children.

text.NRSV: Thus it shall be done to you, O Bethel, because of your great wickedness. At dawn the king of Israel shall be utterly cut off.
text.Rotherham: Even so, hath Bethel, done to you, because of your exceeding wickedness,—in the dawn, shall the king of Israel be, utterly silenced.
text.KJV: So shall Beth–el do unto you because of your great wickedness: in a morning shall the king of Israel utterly be cut off.
text.NABRE: So it will be done to you, Bethel, because of your utter wickedness: At dawn the king of Israel will utterly disappear.
text.DRB: So hath Bethel done to you, because of the malice of your iniquities.

text.NRSV: When Israel was a child, I loved him, and out of Egypt I called my son.
text.Rotherham: When Israel was, a child, then I loved him,—and, out of Egypt, called I my son.
text.KJV: When Israel was a child, then I loved him, and called my son out of Egypt.
text.NABRE: When Israel was a child I loved him, out of Egypt I called my son.
text.DRB: As the morning passed, hath the king of Israel passed away. Because Israel was a child, and I loved him: & out of Ægypt I called my son.

text.NRSV: The more I called them, the more they went from me; they kept sacrificing to the Baals, and offering incense to idols.
text.Rotherham: They invited them,—at once, they departed from before me, they, to the Baals, sacrificed, and, to the images, offered incense.
text.KJV: As they called them, so they went from them: they sacrificed unto Baalim, and burned incense to graven images.
text.NABRE: The more I called them, the farther they went from me, Sacrificing to the Baals and burning incense to idols.
text.DRB: They called them, so they departed from their face: they immolated to Baalim, and sacrificed to idols.

text.NRSV: Yet it was I who taught Ephraim to walk, I took them up in my arms; but they did not know that I healed them.
text.Rotherham: Yet, I, had taught Ephraim to walk, I used to take them upon mine arms,—But they acknowledged not that I had healed them.
text.KJV: I taught Ephraim also to go, taking them by their arms; but they knew not that I healed them.
text.NABRE: Yet it was I who taught Ephraim to walk, who took them in my arms; but they did not know that I cared for them.
text.DRB: And I as it were the nurse of Ephraim, carried them in mine arms: and they knew not that I cured them.

text.NRSV: I led them with cords of human kindness, with bands of love. I was to them like those who lift infants to their cheeks. I bent down to them and fed them.
text.Rotherham: With human cords, used I to draw them, with the bands of love, so became I unto them like those who remove the yoke [that was] on their jaws,—and, holding out [food] to him, I let him eat.
text.KJV: I drew them with cords of a man, with bands of love: and I was to them as they that take off the yoke on their jaws, and I laid meat unto them.
text.NABRE: I drew them with human cords, with bands of love; I fostered them like those who raise an infant to their cheeks; I bent down to feed them.
text.DRB: In the cords of Adam I will draw them, in the bands of charity: and I will be to them as lifting up the yoke upon their cheeks: and I declined to him that he might eat.

text.NRSV: They shall return to the land of Egypt, and Assyria shall be their king, because they have refused to return to me.
text.Rotherham: He was not to turn back into the land of Egypt, Howbeit, the Assyrian—he, became his king, for they refused to turn.
text.KJV: He shall not return into the land of Egypt, but the Assyrian shall be his king, because they refused to return.
text.NABRE: He shall return to the land of Egypt, Assyria shall be his king, because they have refused to repent.
text.DRB: He shall not return into the Land of Ægypt, and Assur he his king: because they would not convert.

text.NRSV: The sword rages in their cities, it consumes their oracle-staves, and devours because of their schemes.
text.Rotherham: Therefore shall the sword, rage, in his cities, and make an end of his multitudes, and consume them,—because of their counsels.
text.KJV: And the sword shall abide on his cities, and shall consume his branches, and devour them, because of their own counsels.
text.NABRE: The sword shall rage in his cities: it shall destroy his diviners, and devour them because of their schemings.
text.DRB: The sword hath begun in his cities, and it shall consume his elect, and shall eat their heads.

text.NRSV: My people are bent on turning away from me. To the Most High they call, but he does not raise them up at all.
text.Rotherham: But, my people, are bent towards turning from me, though upwards they call them, none of them can lift them.
text.KJV: And my people are bent to backsliding from me: though they called them to the most High, none at all would exalt him.
text.NABRE: My people have their mind set on apostasy; though they call on God in unison, he shall not raise them up.
text.DRB: And my people shall hang upon my return: but a yoke shall be put upon them together, which shall not be taken away.

text.NRSV: How can I give you up, Ephraim? How can I hand you over, O Israel? How can I make you like Admah? How can I treat you like Zeboiim? My heart recoils within me; my compassion grows warm and tender.
text.Rotherham: How can I give thee up, Ephraim? abandon thee Israel? How can I make thee as Admah? set thee as Zeboim? Mine own heart, turneth against me, at once, are kindled my compassions.
text.KJV: How shall I give thee up, Ephraim? how shall I deliver thee, Israel? how shall I make thee as Admah? how shall I set thee as Zeboim? mine heart is turned within me, my repentings are kindled together.
text.NABRE: But Love Is Stronger and Restores How could I give you up, Ephraim, or deliver you up, Israel? How could I treat you as Admah, or make you like Zeboiim? My heart is overwhelmed, my pity is stirred.
text.DRB: How shall I give thee Ephraim, protect thee Israel? How shall I give thee as Adama, lay thee as Seboim? My heart is turned within, my repentance is disturbed together.

text.NRSV: I will not execute my fierce anger; I will not again destroy Ephraim; for I am God and no mortal, the Holy One in your midst, and I will not come in wrath.
text.Rotherham: I cannot execute the glow of mine anger, I cannot turn to destroy Ephraim,—for, GOD, am, I, and not man, When thou drawest near, [I am] a Holy One, though I do not enter a city.
text.KJV: I will not execute the fierceness of mine anger, I will not return to destroy Ephraim: for I am God, and not man; the Holy One in the midst of thee: and I will not enter into the city.
text.NABRE: I will not give vent to my blazing anger, I will not destroy Ephraim again; For I am God and not a man, the Holy One present among you; I will not come in wrath.
text.DRB: I will not do the fury of my wrath: I will not return to destroy Ephraim: because I am God, and not man: in the midst of thee the Holy one, and I will not enter into the city.

text.NRSV: They shall go after the Lord, who roars like a lion; when he roars, his children shall come trembling from the west.
text.Rotherham: After Yahweh, let them go, Like a lion, will he roar,—When, he, shall roar, then let sons, come trembling, out of the West.
text.KJV: They shall walk after the Lord: he shall roar like a lion: when he shall roar, then the children shall tremble from the west.
text.NABRE: They shall follow the Lord, who roars like a lion; When he roars, his children shall come frightened from the west,
text.DRB: They shall walk after our Lord, as a lion will he roar: because he will roar, and the children of the sea shall fear.

text.NRSV: They shall come trembling like birds from Egypt, and like doves from the land of Assyria; and I will return them to their homes, says the Lord.
text.Rotherham: Let them come trembling like a small bird out of Egypt, and like a dove out of the land of Assyria,—so will I cause them to dwell by their own houses, Declareth Yahweh.
text.KJV: They shall tremble as a bird out of Egypt, and as a dove out of the land of Assyria: and I will place them in their houses, saith the Lord.
text.NABRE: Out of Egypt they shall come trembling, like birds, like doves, from the land of Assyria; And I will resettle them in their homes, oracle of the Lord.
text.DRB: And they shall fly away as a bird out of Ægypt, and as a dove out of the Land of the Assyrians: and I will place them in their houses, saith our Lord.

text.NRSV: Ephraim has surrounded me with lies, and the house of Israel with deceit; but Judah still walks with God, and is faithful to the Holy One.
text.Rotherham: They have compassed me about—with denial, Ephraim, with deceit, the house of Israel,—but, Judah, hath, again and again, run riot with GOD, though, with the holy places, entrusted.
text.KJV: Ephraim compasseth me about with lies, and the house of Israel with deceit: but Judah yet ruleth with God, and is faithful with the saints.
text.DRB: Ephraim hath compassed me in denying, and the house of Israel in deceit: but Judas a witness is descended with God, and with the saints, faithful.

text.NRSV: Ephraim herds the wind, and pursues the east wind all day long; they multiply falsehood and violence; they make a treaty with Assyria, and oil is carried to Egypt.
text.Rotherham: Ephraim, feedeth on wind, and pursueth the east wind, all the day, falsehood and force, doth he magnify,—and, a covenant with Assyria, would they solemnize, and, oil into Egypt, must be borne along.
text.KJV: Ephraim feedeth on wind, and followeth after the east wind: he daily increaseth lies and desolation; and they do make a covenant with the Assyrians, and oil is carried into Egypt.
text.NABRE: Ephraim has surrounded me with lies, the house of Israel, with deceit; Judah still wanders about with gods, and is faithful to holy ones.
text.DRB: Ephraim feedeth the wind, and followeth the heat: all the day he multiplieth lying and waste: and he hath made a league with the Assyrians, and he carried oil into Ægypt.

text.NRSV: The Lord has an indictment against Judah, and will punish Jacob according to his ways, and repay him according to his deeds.
text.Rotherham: But, a controversy, hath Yahweh with Judah,—so that he may bring punishment on Jacob, according to his ways, According to his doings, repay him.
text.KJV: The Lord hath also a controversy with Judah, and will punish Jacob according to his ways; according to his doings will he recompense him.
text.NABRE: Ephraim shepherds the wind, and pursues the east wind all day long. He multiplies lies and violence: They make a covenant with Assyria, and oil is carried to Egypt.
text.DRB: The judgement therefore of our Lord with Juda, and visitation upon Jacob: according to his ways, & according to his inventions he will render to him.

text.NRSV: In the womb he tried to supplant his brother, and in his manhood he strove with God.
text.Rotherham: In the womb, took he his brother by the heel; and, in his manly vigour, strove he with God:
text.KJV: He took his brother by the heel in the womb, and by his strength he had power with God:
text.NABRE: The Lord has a dispute with Judah, and will punish Jacob for his conduct, and repay him for his deeds.
text.DRB: In the womb he supplanted his brother: and in his strength he was directed with the Angel.

text.NRSV: He strove with the angel and prevailed, he wept and sought his favor; he met him at Bethel, and there he spoke with him.
text.Rotherham: Yea he strove against a Messenger, and prevailed, he wept, and made supplication unto him,—At Bethel, he found him, and, there, he spake with us;
text.KJV: Yea, he had power over the angel, and prevailed: he wept, and made supplication unto him: he found him in Beth–el, and there he spake with us;
text.NABRE: In the womb he supplanted his brother, and in his vigor he contended with a divine being;
text.DRB: And he prevailed against the Angel, and was strengthened: and he wept, and besought him: in Bethel he found him, and there he spake with us.

text.NRSV: The Lord the God of hosts, the Lord is his name!
text.Rotherham: And, Yahweh, is God of host,—Yahweh, is his memorial.
text.KJV: Even the Lord God of hosts; the Lord is his memorial.
text.NABRE: He contended with an angel and prevailed, he wept and entreated him. At Bethel he met with him, and there he spoke with him.
text.DRB: And our Lord the God of hosts, the Lord is his memorial.

text.NRSV: But as for you, return to your God, hold fast to love and justice, and wait continually for your God.
text.Rotherham: Thou, therefore, by thy God, shalt return,—lovingkindness and justice, do thou keep, so wait thou for thy God, continually.
text.KJV: Therefore turn thou to thy God: keep mercy and judgment, and wait on thy God continually.
text.NABRE: The Lord is the God of hosts, the Lord is his name!
text.DRB: And thou shalt convert to thy God: keep mercy and judgement, & hope in thy God always.

text.NRSV: A trader, in whose hands are false balances, he loves to oppress.
text.Rotherham: A trafficker! in his hand, are balances of deceit, to oppress, he loveth.
text.KJV: He is a merchant, the balances of deceit are in his hand: he loveth to oppress.
text.NABRE: You must return to your God. Maintain loyalty and justice and always hope in your God.
text.DRB: Chanaan in his hand a deceitful balance, he hath loved calumny.

text.NRSV: Ephraim has said, “Ah, I am rich, I have made a fortune for myself; in all my gain no offense has been found in me that would be sin.”
text.Rotherham: So then Ephraim said, Surely I have gotten me riches, I have found wealth for myself,—in all my toils, they cannot find in me perversity which is sin.
text.KJV: And Ephraim said, Yet I am become rich, I have found me out substance: in all my labours they shall find none iniquity in me that were sin.
text.NABRE: A merchant who holds a false balance, he loves to extort!
text.DRB: And Ephraim said: But yet I am made rich, I have found an idol to myself: all my labours shall not find me the iniquity, which I have sinned.

text.NRSV: I am the Lord your God from the land of Egypt; I will make you live in tents again, as in the days of the appointed festival.
text.Rotherham: But, I, Yahweh, have been thy God, from the land of Egypt,—I will yet make thee dwell in tents, as in the days of appointed meeting.
text.KJV: And I that am the Lord thy God from the land of Egypt will yet make thee to dwell in tabernacles, as in the days of the solemn feast.
text.NABRE: Ephraim has said, “How rich I have become; I have made a fortune!” All his gain will not suffice for the guilt of his sin.
text.DRB: And I the Lord thy God out of the Land of Ægypt I will yet make thee sit in tabernacles, as in the days of festivity.

text.NRSV: I spoke to the prophets; it was I who multiplied visions, and through the prophets I will speak parables.
text.Rotherham: And I will lay my word upon the prophets, yea, I myself, have magnified, vision,—and, by the hand of the prophets, will I use similitudes.
text.KJV: I have also spoken by the prophets, and I have multiplied visions, and used similitudes, by the ministry of the prophets.
text.NABRE: I the Lord have been your God, since the land of Egypt; I will again have you live in tents, as on feast days.
text.DRB: And I have spoken upon the Prophets, and I have multiplied vision, and in the hand of the Prophets I have been resembled.

text.NRSV: In Gilead there is iniquity, they shall surely come to nothing. In Gilgal they sacrifice bulls, so their altars shall be like stone-heaps in the furrows of the field.
text.Rotherham: If, Gilead, is in sorrow, surely false, have they been, In Gilgal, have they sacrificed, bullocks,—their very altars, shall become as heaps upon the furrows of the field.
text.KJV: Is there iniquity in Gilead? surely they are vanity: they sacrifice bullocks in Gilgal; yea, their altars are as heaps in the furrows of the fields.
text.NABRE: I spoke to the prophets, I granted many visions, and through the prophets I told parables.
text.DRB: If an idol in Galaad, then in vain were they in Galgal immolating with oxen: for their altars also as heaps upon the furrows of the field.

text.NRSV: Jacob fled to the land of Aram, there Israel served for a wife, and for a wife he guarded sheep.
text.Rotherham: When Jacob fled to the country of Syria, then Israel served for a wife, and, for a wife, he watched over a flock.
text.KJV: And Jacob fled into the country of Syria, and Israel served for a wife, and for a wife he kept sheep.
text.NABRE: In Gilead is falsehood, they have come to nothing; in Gilgal they sacrifice bulls, But their altars are like heaps of stones in the furrows of the field.
text.DRB: Jacob fled into the country of Syria, and Israel served for a wife, and for a wife he kept her.

text.NRSV: By a prophet the Lord brought Israel up from Egypt, and by a prophet he was guarded.
text.Rotherham: And, by a prophet, Yahweh, brought up, Israel out of Egypt,—and, by a prophet, was he watched over.
text.KJV: And by a prophet the Lord brought Israel out of Egypt, and by a prophet was he preserved.
text.NABRE: Jacob fled to the land of Aram, and Israel served for a wife; for a wife he tended sheep.
text.DRB: But by a Prophet our Lord brought Israel out of Ægypt: and by a Prophet he was preserved.

text.NRSV: Ephraim has given bitter offense, so his Lord will leave his blood-guilt upon him, and will pay him back for his insults.
text.Rotherham: Ephraim hath provoked, very bitterly,—his own blood, therefore, upon him, will he leave, and, his reproach, shall his Lord, bring back to him.
text.KJV: Ephraim provoked him to anger most bitterly: therefore shall he leave his blood upon him, and his reproach shall his Lord return unto him.
text.NABRE: But by a prophet the Lord brought Israel out of Egypt, and by a prophet Israel was tended.
text.DRB: Ephraim hath provoked me to wrath in his bitterness, and his blood shall come upon him, and his reproach his Lord will restore to him.

text.NRSV: When Ephraim spoke, there was trembling; he was exalted in Israel; but he incurred guilt through Baal and died.
text.Rotherham: When Ephraim, spake, there was terror, exalted was, he, in Israel,—but, when he became guilty with Baal, then he died.
text.KJV: When Ephraim spake trembling, he exalted himself in Israel; but when he offended in Baal, he died.
text.NABRE: When Ephraim spoke there was terror; he was exalted in Israel; but he became guilty through Baal and died.
text.DRB: Ephraim speaking, horror invaded Israel: & he sinned in Baal, and died.

text.NRSV: And now they keep on sinning and make a cast image for themselves, idols of silver made according to their understanding, all of them the work of artisans. “Sacrifice to these,” they say. People are kissing calves!
text.Rotherham: Now, therefore they go on to sin, and have made them a Molten Thing out of their silver, after the notion of idols, the workmanship of craftsmen, all of it! Of them, are they saying—Ye sacrificers of men! The Great Calf, shall ye surely kiss!
text.KJV: And now they sin more and more, and have made them molten images of their silver, and idols according to their own understanding, all of it the work of the craftsmen: they say of them, Let the men that sacrifice kiss the calves.
text.NABRE: Now they continue to sin, making for themselves molten images, Silver idols according to their skill, all of them the work of artisans. “To these, offer sacrifice,” they say. People kiss calves!
text.DRB: And now they have added to sin: and they have made to themselves a molten of their silver as it were the similitude of idols, the whole is the work of crafts-men: to these they say: Immolate men adoring calves.

text.NRSV: Therefore they shall be like the morning cloud or like the dew that goes away early, like chaff that swirls from the threshing floor or like smoke from a window.
text.Rotherham: Therefore, shall they become like the morning cloud, and like the dew early departing,—like chaff storm-driven out of the threshing-floor, and like smoke out of a chimney.
text.KJV: Therefore they shall be as the morning cloud, and as the early dew that passeth away, as the chaff that is driven with the whirlwind out of the floor, and as the smoke out of the chimney.
text.NABRE: Therefore, they will be like a morning cloud or like the dew that vanishes with the dawn, Like chaff storm-driven from the threshing floor or like smoke out of the window.
text.DRB: Therefore they shall be as a morning cloud, & as a morning dew passing away, as dust caught with a whirlwind out of the floor, and as smoke out of the chimney.

text.NRSV: Yet I have been the Lord your God ever since the land of Egypt; you know no God but me, and besides me there is no savior.
text.Rotherham: Yet, I, Yahweh, have been thy God from the land of Egypt,—and, god beside me, shalt thou not acknowledge, for, saviour, is there none besides me.
text.KJV: Yet I am the Lord thy God from the land of Egypt, and thou shalt know no god but me: for there is no saviour beside me.
text.NABRE: I, the Lord, am your God, since the land of Egypt; Gods apart from me you do not know; there is no savior but me.
text.DRB: But I the Lord thy God out of the Land of Ægypt: and God beside me thou shalt not know, and there is no Saviour beside me.

text.NRSV: It was I who fed you in the wilderness, in the land of drought.
text.Rotherham: I, tended thee in the desert,—in a land parched with drought:
text.KJV: I did know thee in the wilderness, in the land of great drought.
text.NABRE: I fed you in the wilderness, in the parched land.
text.DRB: I knew thee in the desert, in the land of wilderness.

text.NRSV: When I fed them, they were satisfied; they were satisfied, and their heart was proud; therefore they forgot me.
text.Rotherham: Whenever they were pastured, then were they satisfied, They were satisfied, and their heart, was lifted up,—because of this, they forgat me.
text.KJV: According to their pasture, so were they filled; they were filled, and their heart was exalted; therefore have they forgotten me.
text.NABRE: When I fed them, they were satisfied; when satisfied, they became proud, therefore they forgot me.
text.DRB: According to their pastures they were filled, and were made full: they have lifted up their heart, and have forgotten me.

text.NRSV: So I will become like a lion to them, like a leopard I will lurk beside the way.
text.Rotherham: Therefore am I become to them as a lion,—As a leopard by the way, do I watch.
text.KJV: Therefore I will be unto them as a lion: as a leopard by the way will I observe them:
text.NABRE: So, I will be like a lion to them, like a leopard by the road I will keep watch.
text.DRB: And I will be unto them as a lioness, as a leopard in the way of the Assyrians.

text.NRSV: I will fall upon them like a bear robbed of her whelps, and will tear open the covering of their heart; there I will devour them like a lion, as a wild animal would mangle them.
text.Rotherham: I will fall upon them as a bear bereaved, and will read asunder the enclosure of their heart,—that I may devour them there, like a lioness, the wild beast of the field, shall tear them in pieces.
text.KJV: I will meet them as a bear that is bereaved of her whelps, and will rend the caul of their heart, and there will I devour them like a lion: the wild beast shall tear them.
text.NABRE: I will attack them like a bear robbed of its young, and tear their hearts from their breasts; I will devour them on the spot like a lion, as a wild animal would rip them open.
text.DRB: I will meet them as a bear her young being violently taken away, and I will break in sunder the inner parts of their liver: and will consume them there as a lion, the beast of the field shall tear them.

text.NRSV: I will destroy you, O Israel; who can help you?
text.Rotherham: It hath utterly destroyed thee, O Israel, for it was against me, as thy helper!
text.KJV: O Israel, thou hast destroyed thyself; but in me is thine help.
text.NABRE: I destroy you, Israel! who is there to help you?
text.DRB: Perdition is thine o Israel: only in me thy help.

text.NRSV: Where now is your king, that he may save you? Where in all your cities are your rulers, of whom you said, “Give me a king and princes”?
text.Rotherham: Where is thy king, then, that he may save thee throughout all thy cities? and thy judges, concerning whom thou saidst, Oh give me a king and rulers?
text.KJV: I will be thy king: where is any other that may save thee in all thy cities? and thy judges of whom thou saidst, Give me a king and princes?
text.NABRE: Where now is your king, that he may rescue you? And all your princes, that they may defend you? Of whom you said, “Give me a king and princes”?
text.DRB: Where is thy king? Now especially let him save thee in all thy cities: and thy judges, of whom thou saidst: Give me kings, and princes.

text.NRSV: I gave you a king in my anger, and I took him away in my wrath.
text.Rotherham: I might give thee a king in mine anger, and take him away in my wrath.
text.KJV: I gave thee a king in mine anger, and took him away in my wrath.
text.NABRE: I give you a king in my anger, and I take him away in my wrath.
text.DRB: I will give thee a king in my fury, and will take him away in mine indignation.

text.NRSV: Ephraim’s iniquity is bound up; his sin is kept in store.
text.Rotherham: Bound up, is the iniquity of Ephraim, stored away, his sin.
text.KJV: The iniquity of Ephraim is bound up; his sin is hid.
text.NABRE: The guilt of Ephraim is wrapped up, his sin is stored away.
text.DRB: The iniquity of Ephraim is bound together, his sin is hidden.

text.NRSV: The pangs of childbirth come for him, but he is an unwise son; for at the proper time he does not present himself at the mouth of the womb.
text.Rotherham: The pangs of a woman in labour, shall overtake him,—he, is a son, not wise, for, now, he cannot stand still, when children are about to be born.
text.KJV: The sorrows of a travailing woman shall come upon him: he is an unwise son; for he should not stay long in the place of the breaking forth of children.
text.NABRE: The birth pangs will come for him, but this is an unwise child, Who, when it is time, does not present himself at the mouth of the womb.
text.DRB: The sorrows of a woman in travail shall come to him, he a son not wise: for now he shall not stand the confraction of the children.

text.NRSV: Shall I ransom them from the power of Sheol? Shall I redeem them from Death? O Death, where are your plagues? O Sheol, where is your destruction? Compassion is hidden from my eyes.
text.Rotherham: Out of the hand of hades, will I ransom them, out of death, will I redeem them,—Where is thy pestilence, O death? Where thy plague, O hades? Repentance, shall be hid from mine eyes.
text.KJV: I will ransom them from the power of the grave; I will redeem them from death: O death, I will be thy plagues; O grave, I will be thy destruction: repentance shall be hid from mine eyes.
text.NABRE: Shall I deliver them from the power of Sheol? shall I redeem them from death? Where are your plagues, O death! where is your sting, Sheol! Compassion is hidden from my eyes.
text.DRB: Out of the hand of death I will deliver them, from death I will redeem them: I will be thy death o death, thy bite will I be o hell, consolation is hidden from mine eyes.

text.NRSV: Although he may flourish among rushes, the east wind shall come, a blast from the Lord, rising from the wilderness; and his fountain shall dry up, his spring shall be parched. It shall strip his treasury of every precious thing.
text.Rotherham: Though, he, among brethren, be fruitful, there shall come in an east wind, the blast of Yahweh out of the desert coming up, that his spring, may dry up, and his fountain, be exhausted, he, will rob the treasure-house of all the vessels of delight.
text.KJV: Though he be fruitful among his brethren, an east wind shall come, the wind of the Lord shall come up from the wilderness, and his spring shall become dry, and his fountain shall be dried up: he shall spoil the treasure of all pleasant vessels.
text.NABRE: Though Ephraim may flourish among his brothers, an east wind will come, a wind from the Lord, rising from the wilderness, That will dry up his spring, and leave his fountain dry. It will loot his treasury of every precious thing.
text.DRB: Because he shall divide between brethren: our Lord will bring a burning wind rising from the desert: and it shall dry up his veins, and shall make his fountain desolate, and he shall spoil the treasure of every vessel that is to be desired.

text.NRSV: Samaria shall bear her guilt, because she has rebelled against her God; they shall fall by the sword, their little ones shall be dashed in pieces, and their pregnant women ripped open.
text.Rotherham: Samaria, shall be held guilty, for she hath rebelled against her God, By the sword, shall they fall, their infants, shall be dashed to the ground, and, his women with child, shall be ripped up.
text.KJV: Samaria shall become desolate; for she hath rebelled against her God: they shall fall by the sword: their infants shall be dashed in pieces, and their women with child shall be ripped up.
text.DRB: Let Samaria perish, because she hath stirred up her God to bitterness: let them perish by the sword, let their little ones be dashed, and let the women with child be cut in sunder.

text.NRSV: Return, O Israel, to the Lord your God, for you have stumbled because of your iniquity.
text.Rotherham: Return thou, O Israel, unto Yahweh thy God,—for thou hast stumbled by thine iniquity.
text.KJV: O Israel, return unto the Lord thy God; for thou hast fallen by thine iniquity.
text.NABRE: They shall fall by the sword, their infants shall be dashed to pieces, their pregnant women shall be ripped open.
text.DRB: Convert o Israel to our Lord thy God: because thou art fallen in thine iniquity.

text.NRSV: Take words with you and return to the Lord; say to him, “Take away all guilt; accept that which is good, and we will offer the fruit of our lips.
text.Rotherham: Take with you words, and return to Yahweh: say unto him—Wholly, shalt thou take away iniquity, Accept, then, with favour, and we will make good the boldness of our lips!
text.KJV: Take with you words, and turn to the Lord: say unto him, Take away all iniquity, and receive us graciously: so will we render the calves of our lips.
text.NABRE: Sincere Conversion and New Life Return, Israel, to the Lord, your God; you have stumbled because of your iniquity.
text.DRB: Take words with you, and convert to our Lord, and say to him: Take away all iniquity, and receive good: and we will render the calves of our lips.

text.NRSV: Assyria shall not save us; we will not ride on horses; we will say no more, ‘Our God,’ to the work of our hands. In you the orphan finds mercy.”
text.Rotherham: Assyria, shall not save us, Upon horses, will we not ride, neither will we say any more—Our god! to the work of our own hands! For, in thee, shall the fatherless, find compassion.
text.KJV: Asshur shall not save us; we will not ride upon horses: neither will we say any more to the work of our hands, Ye are our gods: for in thee the fatherless findeth mercy.
text.NABRE: Take with you words, and return to the Lord; Say to him, “Forgive all iniquity, and take what is good. Let us offer the fruit of our lips.
text.DRB: Assur shall not save us, we will not mount upon horse: neither will we say any more: Our gods the work of our hands: because thou wilt have mercy on the pupil, which is in thee.

text.NRSV: I will heal their disloyalty; I will love them freely, for my anger has turned from them.
text.Rotherham: I will heal their apostacy, I will love them freely,—for mine anger, hath turned, from them.
text.KJV: I will heal their backsliding, I will love them freely: for mine anger is turned away from him.
text.NABRE: Assyria will not save us, nor will we mount horses; We will never again say, ‘Our god,’ to the work of our hands; for in you the orphan finds compassion.”
text.DRB: I will heal their confractions, I will love them voluntarily: because my fury is averted from them.

text.NRSV: I will be like the dew to Israel; he shall blossom like the lily, he shall strike root like the forests of Lebanon.
text.Rotherham: I will become as the dew unto Israel, he shall break forth as the lily,—and he shall strike his roots as Lebanon:
text.KJV: I will be as the dew unto Israel: he shall grow as the lily, and cast forth his roots as Lebanon.
text.NABRE: I will heal their apostasy, I will love them freely; for my anger is turned away from them.
text.DRB: I will be as dew, Israel shall spring as the lily, and his root shall break forth as that of Libanus.

text.NRSV: His shoots shall spread out; his beauty shall be like the olive tree, and his fragrance like that of Lebanon.
text.Rotherham: His branches, shall spread, that, like an olive-tree, may be his fresh beauty,—and his fragrance, like Lebanon.
text.KJV: His branches shall spread, and his beauty shall be as the olive tree, and his smell as Lebanon.
text.NABRE: I will be like the dew for Israel: he will blossom like the lily; He will strike root like the Lebanon cedar,
text.DRB: His boughs shall go, and his glory shall be as the olive-tree: and his smell as of Libanus.

text.NRSV: They shall again live beneath my shadow, they shall flourish as a garden; they shall blossom like the vine, their fragrance shall be like the wine of Lebanon.
text.Rotherham: They who dwell in his shade shall again show life like the corn, and break forth as the vine,—and, the remembrance of him, shall be like the wine of Lebanon.
text.KJV: They that dwell under his shadow shall return; they shall revive as the corn, and grow as the vine: the scent thereof shall be as the wine of Lebanon.
text.NABRE: and his shoots will go forth. His splendor will be like the olive tree and his fragrance like Lebanon cedar.
text.DRB: They shall be converted that sit under his shadow: they shall live with wheat, and they shall spring as a vine: his memorial as the wine of Libanus.

text.NRSV: O Ephraim, what have I to do with idols? It is I who answer and look after you. I am like an evergreen cypress; your faithfulness comes from me.
text.Rotherham: Ephraim [saith] —What to me any more are idols? I, have answered, and have closely observed him, I, am like a fir-tree that is green, From me, is thy fruit found.
text.KJV: Ephraim shall say, What have I to do any more with idols? I have heard him, and observed him: I am like a green fir tree. From me is thy fruit found.
text.NABRE: Again they will live in his shade; they will raise grain, They will blossom like the vine, and his renown will be like the wine of Lebanon.
text.DRB: Ephraim, what have I to do any more with idols? I will hear, and I will direct him as a verdant fir-tree: out of me thy fruit is found.

text.NRSV: Those who are wise understand these things; those who are discerning know them. For the ways of the Lord are right, and the upright walk in them, but transgressors stumble in them.
text.Rotherham: Who is wise, that he may understand these things? intelligent, that he may take knowledge of them? For, straightforward, are the ways of Yahweh, and, the righteous, shall travel therein, but, transgressors, shall stumble therein.
text.KJV: Who is wise, and he shall understand these things? prudent, and he shall know them? for the ways of the Lord are right, and the just shall walk in them: but the transgressors shall fall therein.
text.NABRE: Ephraim! What more have I to do with idols? I have humbled him, but I will take note of him. I am like a verdant cypress tree. From me fruit will be found for you!
text.DRB: Who is wise, and shall understand these things? Of understanding, and shall know these things? Because the ways of our Lord be right, and the just shall walk in them: but prevaricators shall fall in them.

1.10: Chapter 2 The number of the Israelites will be like the sand of the sea, which can be neither measured nor counted. Instead of being told, “You are Not-My-People,” They will be called, “Children of the living God.”
1.11: Then the people of Judah and of Israel will gather together; They will appoint for themselves one head and rise up from the land; great indeed shall be the day of Jezreel!

11.12: Chapter 12 Infidelity of Israel Ephraim has surrounded me with lies, the house of Israel, with deceit; Judah still wanders about with gods, and is faithful to holy ones.

13.16: Chapter 14 Samaria has become guilty, for she has rebelled against her God. They shall fall by the sword, their infants shall be dashed to pieces, their pregnant women shall be ripped open.
